title: 古琴古曲｜《离骚》：“始则抑郁，继则豪爽”
description:
演奏：孔子文
打谱、传承：李凤云
录音：曹晓冬
录像、妆造：三人照相馆

《离骚》，此曲为晚唐琴家陈康士据屈原辞所作，由当代琴家李凤云据《神奇秘谱》打谱。《离骚》共十八拍，每拍都以屈原的诗句作为小标题。
这首乐曲的用音不局限于五声音阶，曲中丰富的变音赋予了乐曲神秘、自由的气质。乐曲由泛音段落开篇，情绪抑郁、显隐莫测，而后逐渐转变为悲愁交加，时而豪爽，时而激愤……
《琴学初津》对此曲评价为“始则抑郁，继则豪爽”，实在贴切。

Encountering Sorrow (handed down by Li Fengyun)

This one was composed by the late Tang guqin player Chen Kangshi based on Qu Yuan’s poem, and constructed by the contemporary guqin player Li Fengyun as per Mysterious Qin Score. Encountering Sorrow involves eighteen beats, each of which is subtitled with a line from Qu Yuan’s poem.
The melody is not limited to the pentatonic scale. Variations of sounds give it a mysterious and free style. It begins with floating sounds, depressing and inscrutable, and then it gradually turns into a mixture of sadness and distress, sometimes cordial and sometimes furious...
It is aptly described as “melancholy at the beginning, followed by boldness” in Qinxue Chujin.

title: 古琴名曲: 郭关《归去来辞》/ Traditional Chinese Music:  Guqin “Come Away Home”: GUO Guan
description:
古琴名曲《归去来辞》，《真传正宗琴谱》，刘景韶传谱，郭关演奏。
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KiB0RNDXzPo

古琴名曲《归去来辞》，其来源为晋代诗人陶渊明的《归去来兮辞》。此曲流传广泛，《杏庄太音补遗》、《文会堂琴谱》、《风宣玄品》、《谢琳太古遗音》、《真传正宗琴谱琴学丛书》等二十多部琴谱都撰刊了此曲。《谢琳太古遗音》琴谱中关于此曲的解析为：“是辞乃晋处士陶潜所作也。潜为彭泽令，郡遣督邮至，军吏白，应束带见之。潜曰，我不能为五斗米折腰。即日解印绶归。作此辞，播之弦歌，令人淸风凛然，千载之下尚能使人兴起也。”

陶渊明的《归去来兮辞》是中国文学史上表现归隐意识的巅峰之作。在这篇辞赋中，陶渊明以真挚的情感和优美的文笔，抒发了他对官场的失望和对自由生活的向往。然而，陶渊明的归隐并非简单的逃避现实，而是一种对生命本质的深刻思考和对内心真实的追求。

在现代社会中，“归隐”这一概念被赋予了新的内涵。我们不必像陶渊明那样完全脱离世俗社会，而是可以在繁忙的都市生活中寻找内心的宁静。现代人的“归隐”更多是一种精神上的独立和心灵上的自由。我们可以在喧嚣中保持内心的平静，在忙碌中找到生活的真谛。

陶渊明的“归隐”思想对现代人仍有重要的启示。在快节奏的现代社会中，我们常常被各种外在的压力和期望所束缚，失去了对内心真实的关注。“富贵非吾愿，帝乡不可期。”这句话提醒我们，真正的幸福不在于外在的名利，而在于内心的满足和平静。

郭关, 著名画家、古琴家。生于湖南桂东，道家全真派道士。作品涉及书画、音乐、诗文、斫琴等领域。七岁始随祖父学习古琴，后受教于龚一、成公亮等，打谱作曲上百首，尺八师从于三桥贵风先生；书法师从于孙伯翔先生；二零零六年闭关参禅一年，出关后画风大变；二零一三年修道；曾就学于北京师范大学、人民大学宗教哲学硕士；著有《郭关诗文集》《郭关绘画》《郭关诗画集》《郭关古琴》等。

title: 没有比这个更孤独的琴曲了 You can feel the enormous loneliness in this guqin song｜泛沧浪
description:
《水调歌头•壬子三山被召陈端仁给事饮饯席上作》
宋·辛弃疾
       长恨复长恨，裁作短歌行。何人为我楚舞，听我楚狂声？余既滋兰九畹，又树蕙之百亩，秋菊更餐英。门外沧浪水，可以濯吾缨。
        一杯酒，问何似，身后名？人间万事，毫发常重泰山轻。悲莫悲生离别，乐莫乐新相识，儿女古今情。富贵非吾事，归与白鸥盟。

《泛沧浪》为南宋古琴大家郭楚望所作，是《潇湘水云》的序曲，虽表现风格各异，但其情感内核却相通：“志在扁舟五湖，弃功名如遗芥。载风月而播弄云水，渺世事之若浮沤。抡竿网罟，日沉天晓，伴烟雨而友鸥鹭。道宏古今，心合太虚。”

我们将它重新编曲演绎，这也是琴为何物·宋·水云归专场的保留曲目之一，视频录制场次是23年苏州湾首演现场~

Fan Cang Lang—Boating Along the Waves 泛沧浪

The music originates from “The Magic Secret Music Score” by Guo Chuwang, a guqin composer of the Southern Song Dynasty. He would often ride his boat across the waves of the five lakes, enjoying the water, the gulls, fishing, nature, and the elements.

title: "Beijing: More Than Meets the Eye"—Guqin: Sound of Utmost Antiquity
description:
During the opening ceremony of the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games, a guqin called “Sounds of Utmost Antiquity” made a stunning appearance. This particular guqin is made by Wang Peng, a contemporary Chinese guqin master. It’s a replica of Shi Kuang-style qin, based on documents from the Tang Dynasty. Now it is among the permanent collection of Juntianfang Guqin Art Center in Daxing, Beijing. As one of the oldest plucked instruments, and with a history of over 3,000 years, guqin was listed as a UNESCO intangible cultural heritage in 2003. The guqin was one of the four arts – along with chess, calligraphy and painting – that Chinese scholars were expected to master in ancient times. In this episode of “Beijing: More Than Meets the Eye,” we will learn about the basics of this instrument, tour around this special guqin museum and appreciate the work of art by Wang Peng, accompanied by the master piece of music entitled “Wild Geese Descending on the Beach.”

title: The Best 10 Traditional Chinese Music of Guqin #relaxingmusic #relaxing
description:
Guqin (pronounced goo-chin) is a traditional seven-stringed plucked instrument from China, with over 3,000 years of history.
It is often referred to as the "Instrument of the Sages" due to its association with Chinese scholars, philosophers, and literati. The guqin is not only a musical instrument but also a symbol of Chinese intellectual and cultural refinement, representing harmony, balance, and introspection in traditional Chinese aesthetics. Known for its delicate, ethereal sound and profound cultural symbolism, the guqin was recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2003.

Here, I have collected ten famous guqin pieces. They are performed by renowned guqin masters and represent the finest versions I could gather.
Among them, I highly recommend the first piece, "Guangling San". This piece carries a legendary aura, telling the story of an ancient assassin seeking revenge for his father. It has long been cherished by scholars and intellectuals of ancient China who valued solitude and transcendence.
At one point, "Guangling San" was lost to history. The version we have today is a modern interpretation based on an ancient musical score.

title: 【古琴Guqin】《平沙落雁》借大雁之远志，抒逸士之心胸 | 自得琴社
description:
《平沙落雁》借大雁之远志，抒逸士之心胸
此曲最早见于明代《古音正宗》（1634年），名为《雁落平沙》。虽出现较晚，却是近三百年来最为流行的曲目，刊载谱集达五十六种，曲谱多达百种。
现今演奏的《平沙落雁》版本虽多，但弹奏最多、影响最广的当属张子谦所传的《蕉庵琴谱》本和管平湖所弹的《琴学丛书》本，本次蔡珊老师将教授的版本为张子谦先生的演奏版本，即“南平沙”。
“南平沙”的处理极具广陵派特色，注重左手吟猱绰注与右手音色的变化，形成了丰富的韵腔，听来恬静平和，韵长意远。而“北平沙”多用密集短小的节奏音型组合，旋律方整、律动感强，左手直来直往、简洁凝练，右手遒劲老辣，气象非凡，志在千里。

演奏：蔡珊
录音/摄影：唐彬

title: 【古琴Guqin】《洞庭秋思》八月湖水平，涵虛混太清 An Autumn tune about missing | 自得琴社
description:
希望在夏天留的遺憾，都能在秋天好好開始。今天是立秋，來一首《洞庭秋思》與你分享！

《洞庭秋思》初見於《西麓堂琴統》（1549年），現流行版本多為查阜西先生據《琴書大全》等諸譜合參打譜而成。此曲古樸儒雅，雋永別致。蕭蕭木葉，萬頃秋波，天光共影，表里俱澄。更有一番滋味，悠然心會，難與君說。

title: 空月｜古琴 古箏 簫 | Empty Moon | Chinese Guqin, Guzheng & Xiao Flute | Relaxation Music & Zen Meditation
description:
《空月》—— 古琴、古箏與簫在夜色下的相遇。
古琴沉靜悠遠，古箏清澈靈動，簫聲空靈如風。三者交織出一幅如水墨般的意境，彷彿在月下遙望，心隨音律漂泊於天地之間。
這首樂曲呈現中國傳統樂器的細膩之美，適合靜靜欣賞、沉澱心靈，體會「空月」的幽遠與空靈。


---

🌙
“Empty Moon” — A meeting of the Guqin, Guzheng, and Xiao flute beneath the night sky.
The Guqin carries deep stillness, the Guzheng sparkles with clarity, and the Xiao flute drifts like a whispering breeze. Together, they weave an atmosphere like ink painting under the moon, inviting the listener to wander between heaven and earth.
This piece highlights the delicate beauty of Chinese traditional instruments — a journey of elegance, tranquility, and poetic resonance.

title: 古琴《菩提心》: 李祥霆/ Chinese Traditional Music, Guqin “Pu Ti Xin (Bodhicitta)”: LI Xiang Ting
description:
古琴《菩提心》: 李祥霆/ Chinese Traditional Music, Guqin “Pu Ti Xin (Bodhicitta)”: LI Xiang Ting
古琴曲《菩提心》，选自古琴大师李祥霆先生的专辑《心》。该专辑在李祥霆先生所理解之佛家精神，所感觉之佛家气象前题下，选定十个题目即兴演奏录制而成，可谓“意至深，理至微，音至乐，明心见性之正音”。

《华严经》所载：“菩提心者，则为一切诸佛种子，能生一切诸佛法故。菩提心者，则为良田，长养众生白净法故。菩提心者，则为大地，能持一切诸世间故。菩提心者，则为净水，洗濯一切烦恼垢故。菩提心者，则为大风，一切世间无障碍故。菩提心者，则为盛火，能烧一切邪见爱故。菩提心者，则为净日，普照一切众生类故。菩提心者，则为明月，诸白净法悉圆满故。”

图片来源: 张大千《观世音造像》

title: Castle in the Sky (天空の城) Theme on Guqin | Kimi wo Nosete (君をのせて) - Ghibli Cover
description:
Let the ancient strings of the Guqin carry you to the magical floating castle of Laputa.

This is my instrumental cover of the beautiful and timeless theme, "Kimi wo Nosete" (Carrying You), from Studio Ghibli's masterpiece, "Castle in the Sky". All credits go to the legendary composer Joe Hisaishi and director Hayao Miyazaki for creating a world that has inspired generations.

I've always wondered how this iconic Japanese melody would sound when played on the Guqin, an ancient Chinese zither with thousands of years of history. This is my attempt to blend two beautiful cultures into one piece of music. I hope you enjoy this moment of peace and nostalgia.

title: 《秋鸿》#Guqin #AutumnGeese #TraditionalChineseMusic #ChineseTraditionalInstruments #GuqinMasterpieces
description:
You’re listening to "Autumn Geese" — a living musical fossil of the Guqin that has been passed down for nearly 800 years.
As one of China’s Top Ten Guqin Masterpieces, it is more than just a piece of music. It is a "picture of wild geese flying south" outlined by musical notes. Guo Chuwang, a Guqin musician from the Southern Song Dynasty, used the autumn wild goose as a metaphor to weave his reflections on heaven, earth, and life into 20 movements. The melody carries both the heroism of geese flapping their wings and the delicacy of a lone goose’s soft cry.
This interpretation strives to restore the charm of the ancient music score:
The harmonic notes under the fingertips mimic the clear sound of geese cutting through the sky.
The slow vibrato hides the distant mood of the autumn river at dusk.
The ebb and flow of the rhythm replicate the geese’s movements — sometimes circling, sometimes flying swiftly.
Put on your headphones and listen to the "goose calls" from a millennium ago travel through time. If you’re also touched by this Eastern artistic conception, feel free to share your thoughts in the comments. Don’t forget to like and subscribe to explore more Chinese stories hidden in Guqin music with us.

description:
Welcome to Feng Channel, a class that only requires diligence and hard work.

First, I will introduce you to a little about this instrument and its history.

According to ancient legends, the first GUQIN had 5 strings. It has been around for a long time, since the time of the Spring and Autumn Periods, people have known about these string instruments. During the reign of King Wen and King Wu of the Chu State, it was improved to have 7 strings.
Its name is GUQIN, you can see that the sound of ku chin is very popular in China and its range is 4 octaves so its range is very wide.

title: Blossoms and Full Moon Night 花好月圓夜 | Classic Guqin Music 古琴经典 | Malam Purnama dan Bunga Mekar #古琴
description:
The Guqin piece "Blossoms and Full Moon Night" (花好月圆夜) captures the beauty and romance of a perfect night where flowers are in full bloom and the moon is radiant. This composition conveys a sense of harmony and joy, symbolizing the traditional Chinese imagery of happiness, reunion, and beauty. The gentle, flowing notes of the Guqin evoke a tranquil atmosphere, reminiscent of festive gatherings under the full moon, where loved ones come together to celebrate the simple yet profound beauty of nature. It invites listeners to savor the elegance of traditional Chinese music and to feel the serenity and warmth of such precious moments.

古琴曲《花好月圓夜》展現了一個花開月圓的完美夜晚的美麗和浪漫，充滿了和諧與喜悅的氛圍。這首曲子表現了中國傳統中幸福、團圓與美好的意象。古琴那輕柔流暢的旋律營造出寧靜的氛圍，彷彿置身於月下花間的節日聚會中，親友相聚，共同欣賞自然的簡單而深邃之美。這首曲子邀請聽者享受中國傳統音樂的優雅，並感受那些彌足珍貴的溫暖時光。

Karya Guqin "Malam Purnama dan Bunga Mekar" (花好月圆夜) menggambarkan keindahan dan romansa malam yang sempurna, di mana bunga-bunga sedang mekar dan bulan bersinar terang. Komposisi ini menyampaikan rasa harmoni dan kebahagiaan, melambangkan gambaran tradisional Tiongkok tentang kebahagiaan, reuni, dan keindahan. Nada-nada lembut dan mengalir dari Guqin menciptakan suasana tenang, mengingatkan pada pertemuan-pertemuan perayaan di bawah bulan purnama, di mana orang-orang terkasih berkumpul untuk merayakan keindahan alam yang sederhana namun mendalam. Lagu ini mengundang pendengar untuk menikmati keanggunan musik tradisional Tiongkok dan merasakan ketenangan serta kehangatan momen-momen berharga tersebut。

title: THE HORSE AND THE MOON | 月下骏马 - Cinematic Guqin & Xiao for Late Night Solitude
description:
🌕 Beneath a silent full moon, a lone horse stands in drifting mist — still, dignified, and timeless.
《月下骏马 | The Horse and the Moon》 is a cinematic traditional Chinese music piece inspired by poetry, solitude, and ink-wash aesthetics.

This composition features solo guqin and xiao, unfolding slowly with spacious pauses and gentle resonance. The music mirrors moonlight itself — calm, reflective, and quietly emotional. Every note breathes with stillness, inviting the listener into a world of ancient night landscapes, bamboo shadows, and unspoken thoughts.

Perfect for late-night listening, meditation, reading, studying, or moments of introspection, this piece belongs to those who seek calm beyond words.

🎵 Instruments: Guqin, Xiao
🌙 Mood: Lonely, serene, introspective
🎨 Visual style: Chinese ink-wash, cinematic moonlight
🕯 Best enjoyed after midnight

Let the moon guide your thoughts, and let the horse stand with you in silence.

title: 这首《桂枝香》好听到流泪Guizhixiang: Beautiful and sad tunes performed by Chinese instruments
description:
《桂枝香》是自得琴社朝代系列音乐会音乐会《琴为何物·宋·水云归》的压轴曲目，倾诉了一段繁华落尽的故国往事，令许多观众动情而落泪。

登临送目，正故国晚秋，天气初肃。千里澄江似练，翠峰如簇。归帆去棹残阳里，背西风，酒旗斜矗。彩舟云淡，星河鹭起，画图难足。
念往昔，繁华竞逐，叹门外楼头，悲恨相续。千古凭高对此，谩嗟荣辱。六朝旧事随流水，但寒烟衰草凝绿。至今商女，时时犹唱，后庭遗曲。
——《桂枝香·金陵怀古》宋·王安石

艺术总监/作曲：唐彬
管弦乐配器：田汨 周楚航
管弦乐录制：布达佩斯交响乐团
演奏：笛子 高雅、二胡 朱婧、古筝 俞辰瑶、琵琶 颜泽、古琴 白无瑕、大鼓 陈曦
调音师：梅卓、吴文隽
舞美设计：郑金启
灯光师：宫徵羽 多媒体：刘孟宸
舞台监督：王砾川、吴蔓妮、游家宁、刘瑷
化妆师：王乔叶、刘蕊 服装/礼仪：王俊涵
导播/剪辑/后期：邱诗浩
摄影师：王恩俊、袁红良、王东波 摄影助理：李腾旋
制作人：朱里钺 谢方 统筹：金卓琪、曾昱丹

title: Ancient Chinese Sleep Music ● 8 Hours ● Guqin & Guzheng ● Deep Sleep & Inner Peace | No Ads
description:
Música tradicional china para dormir 8 horas | Guqin, guzheng, xiao y erhu para paz interior y sueño profundo sin interrupciones

This is not ambient music. This is ancient sound.

8 hours of pure traditional Chinese sleep music — composed exclusively with guqin, guzheng, xiao, dizi, erhu, sheng, ceremonial bells, and soft organic percussion. No western instruments. No modern production. No melodies that demand attention.

Scientifically structured at 68–70 BPM using isochronic principles to gradually slow your nervous system, quiet mental activity, and guide you into the deepest stages of sleep — as naturally as drifting through ancient mist-covered mountains at night.

Visualized through breathtaking 4K drone footage of southern China's karst mountains, sacred valleys, and morning mist — some of the most serene landscapes on Earth.

Perfect for:
✔ Deep sleep and chronic insomnia
✔ Meditation and mindfulness
✔ Nervous system reset and stress relief
✔ Yoga, breathwork and inner stillness
✔ Anyone seeking genuine musical heritage in their sleep practice


This music was designed to feel like drifting through ancient mist-covered mountains at night — weightless, timeless, and deeply restorative.

Subscribe to Sonorest for new sleep sounds and music every week.

title: 【古琴GuqinX昆曲Kunqu】高燃應戰【原神】《神女劈觀》國家隊真·武旦再劈一刀！GENSHIN IMPACT "The Divine Damsel of Devastation"
description:
最近群玉閣上《神女劈觀》的故事演的火熱，從二次元到三次元，竟逐漸演繹成了各劇種八仙過海各顯神通的名場面。粵劇改編後，詞更貼曲，唱來尤顯韻味。昆曲是百戲之祖，綿延存續已六百年，中途歷經魏良輔改良，花雅之爭，曾經唱過村社俚曲，曾經登過大雅之堂。不幸衰落，復又興起，幽蘭雅韻，芬芳獨具。既然百戲盡出，昆腔自不能落後，昆曲是詞曲舞三位一體，原曲尤佳，只惜昆腔難謳原詞，故新翻韻腳，重填新詞以諧律。詞曲已備，舞亦不可缺，有幸邀上海昆劇團青年武旦演員錢瑜婷出演，用《扈家莊》劇中扈三娘的服飾盔帽，借武旦刀重演劈觀故事。武旦亦稱刀馬旦，昆劇行當家門中本無此家，後衍出，專司翻騰打鬥、武藝高強的女性角色。武旦的功夫，可見於耍翎舞刃，英姿颯爽，較溫婉的閨門旦大有不同。

原曲：原神《神女劈觀·喚情》
原唱：楊揚
昆劇演唱/身段：錢瑜婷
古琴：白無瑕
改編填詞：方圳海
化妝：林月媛 盔帽：包驍
錄音/混音/拍攝／後期：唐彬
出品：自得琴社
特別鳴謝：上海昆劇團

《神女劈觀》昆腔重填詞版：
唱：可嘆
秋鴻塞雁恨孤鳶
幾番離恨並仇怨
哀眾生，獻邪祭-招-禍罹難
麗影遽現，仗劍，掃盡——狼煙
紅塵一夢仙緣渺
來世絳府玉名標
紅絲難縛爛柯樵
雲鶴沖天霄
旋鳴百轉意難消
魂幡飄飄天際遙
振羽臨風長嗥
聲悄
（白）往事新曲，唱與諸位聽！
唱：白雪陽春知音識
琴簫翻作訴衷詞
紅纓亂點流星矢
劃盡蒼穹作青史
（白）昔日鶴歸身無匹，今日高朋會滿堂

title: 【古琴Guqin】南宋古曲《列子御风》凌虚御风，神游六合Lieziyufeng: Taoist Guqin music from thousands of years ago
description:
相传为南宋著名琴家毛敏仲所作，估计其创作年代不会晚于南宋。
“是曲乃敏仲翁取《列子·黄帝》篇‘御风凝神游六合’所作，其趣同。列子御风，是以神御虚也，周流六虚，无往不臧，惟至人能之。御风以行若驰天马然，扶摇于九万之上，遨游于六合之中，俯仰乾坤，飞跨宇宙，列迹于清虚无极之表而与天地相为始终焉。”

【卡農】在字面上是「輪唱」的意思。
此曲創作於1680年，是巴洛克時期的音樂作品，

原版由三個小提琴演奏，並以大鍵琴和大提琴及魯特琴伴奏。
它曾被改編為多個不同版本，供不同樂器組合演奏。

此曲移植於古琴演奏家 梅暘，
指法安排恰當、難易度適中 適合任何琴人哦！
再經由我利用卡農形式增添音樂色彩，
用古琴演奏別有一番風味
祝大家聖誕節快樂～！

title: 【古琴Guqin】《烏夜啼》據說是古代的「好運來」
description:
古人以慈烏夜啼為喜兆。《神奇秘譜》題解雲:"按唐書樂誌曰，烏夜啼者，宋臨川王義慶所作也。元嘉十七年，徙彭城王義康於豫章，義慶時為江州剌史，至鎮相見而哭。文帝聞而怪之，徵還宅大驚。伎妾夜聞烏夜啼聲，扣齋閣雲：明日應有赦。其年更為南兗州刺史，因是而作烏夜啼之曲。蓋臨川王之作古樂府耳，非琴操也。大概樂府之於琴操，喻意同耳"。 本曲以繁促的烏啼聲與平靜的泛音旋律作出鮮明的對比，生動體現了緊張不安與泰然自若兩種心境。

title: 琴箫曲: 巫娜《古琴禅修》/ Chinese Guqin & Vertical Bamboo Flute “Meditation by Guqin”: WU Na
description:
琴箫合奏曲《古琴禅修》选自巫娜的专辑《天禅》,广东龙源音像有限公司(2010-12-10)。

佛曰：一花一世界，一木一浮生，一草一天堂，一叶一如来，一砂一极乐，一方一净土，一笑一尘缘，一念一清静。听琴如修禅，古琴空灵、清幽，蕴含天地无限和深微，要用内心的一刻超然和静谧来与「孤高岑寂」、「淡而会心」境界相衬。人生如古琴，愈简愈淡愈通幽、返璞归真，宁静以致远，七弦通古今。
笔墨随风舞、行事若流云，世事皆看淡、唯有箫和鸣。纵然琴南箫北不相闻，亦有琴歌的起，诗言志，歌咏言，声依咏，曲由心。
古琴音乐天生与心灵相通，让我们体会禅的意境，使我们的心灵达到回归生命本真的一种精神境界。

《天禅》是一张充满禅心、禅悟、禅意、禅机、禅定与禅谛的古琴音乐专辑，由巫娜倾力演奏，听着这张专辑，会让人有一种寂静而又意识清明澄澈的心境。盘腿静坐，就能让你的心明净下来，这时候，我们的理想、计划、就会明明朗朗、清清净净，就能懂得如何处理，明白人生究竟是怎么一回事。

title: Cổ cầm có cần đeo móng giả không? #feng #cổ_cầm #guqin
description:
Chào mừng các bạn đến với kênh Feng Channel trong video ngày hôm nay chúng ta hãy cùng đến với: Tại sao chơi Guzheng cần đeo móng giả, còn Guqin thì không?

Why do you need to wear fake nails to play Guzheng, but not Guqin?

Câu hỏi mà các học viên học Guqin thường hay đặt ra nhất có lẽ là: Tại sao khi chơi Guzheng phải đeo móng tay giả, trong khi chơi Guqin thì không cần?

Theo tôi, điều này có thể giải thích ngắn gọn như sau: Guzheng được sử dụng phổ biến trong các buổi biểu diễn vì âm thanh của nó lớn và rõ ràng hơn, và khi đeo móng tay giả, âm thanh sẽ được khuếch đại tốt hơn. Trong khi đó, Guqin thường được chơi trong không gian nhỏ như phòng học hoặc khi chỉ có hai ba người bạn thân cùng thưởng thức. Vì vậy, người chơi Guqin không cần phải tạo ra âm thanh quá lớn và chỉ cần dùng móng tay thật.

Một lý do quan trọng nữa là kỹ thuật chơi Guqin sử dụng hai phương pháp bấm dây: bấm đúp và bấm ngón hướng ra ngoài. Các góc tiếp xúc dây khác nhau cũng như vị trí cao và thấp của ngón tay sẽ tạo ra các hiệu ứng âm sắc khác nhau. Móng tay thật với độ mềm và cứng tự nhiên sẽ giúp tạo ra những âm sắc phong phú và đa dạng hơn. Đây có thể là lý do khiến nhiều người yêu thích Guqin!

title: El guqin y su música
description:
UNESCO: Lista representativa del Patrimonio Cultural Inmaterial de la Humanidad -- 2008
URL: http://www.unesco.org/culture/ich/RL/00020
Descripción: La cítara china, llamada guqin, existe desde hace 3.000 años y ocupa un lugar primordial entre los instrumentos musicales solistas de China. Este instrumento secular, que ya aparece atestado en fuentes literarias antiguas corroboradas por descubrimientos arqueológicos, es indisociable de la historia de los intelectuales chinos. El arte de tocar el guqin estaba reservado originalmente a una elite y lo practicaban en la intimidad nobles y eruditos. Junto con la caligrafía, la pintura y una antigua forma de ajedrez, formaba parte de las cuatro artes que toda persona culta debía dominar. Según la tradición, se necesitan unos veinte años de práctica para llegar a ser un maestro del guqin.
El guqin consta de siete cuerdas y trece posiciones que marcan los tonos. Al colocar las cuerdas de diez maneras distintas, los músicos pueden obtener un conjunto de cuatro octavas. Hay tres técnicas instrumentales básicas: san (cuerda libre), an (cuerda fija) y fan (armónicos). La san se toca pinzando las cuerdas con la mano derecha una por una o en grupos, lo que permite emitir sonidos fuertes y claros para las notas importantes. En la técnica fan los dedos de la mano izquierda rozan suavemente la cuerda en los puntos que indican las marcas incrustadas, mientras que la mano derecha pinza la cuerda, lo que produce un sonido suave y flotante. Para la técnica an también se utilizan ambas manos: la derecha pinza la cuerda, mientras que un dedo de la mano izquierda ejerce una fuerte presión sobre ella y puede deslizarse hasta las otras notas o realizar diversos ornamentos musicales y vibratos.
Hoy día, quedan menos de mil artistas capaces de dominar este instrumento y no más de cincuenta maestros en vida. Del repertorio inicial de varios miles de composiciones, apenas un centenar de obras se interpretan regularmente.

title: Bí mật về giá của một chiếc cổ cầm Guqin | Feng
description:
Chào mừng các bạn đến với kênh Feng Channel trong video ngày hôm nay chúng ta hãy cùng tìm hiểu về Bí mật về giá của một chiếc cổ cầm Guqin nhé

Trong video này, chúng ta sẽ khám phá những yếu tố làm nên giá trị của một cây cổ cầm, từ loại gỗ độc đáo đến bàn tay tài hoa của những nghệ nhân chế tác. Bạn sẽ ngạc nhiên khi biết rằng không chỉ chất liệu mà còn cả kết cấu hộp âm và người thợ làm đàn đóng vai trò quan trọng trong việc tạo nên âm thanh hoàn hảo.

Hơn nữa, sau những biến động lịch sử, số lượng người chơi và chế tác cổ cầm trở nên hiếm hoi, biến những cây đàn được làm bởi các nghệ nhân lão luyện trở thành bảo vật vô giá.

Hãy cùng mình tìm hiểu thêm về câu chuyện đằng sau loại nhạc cụ truyền thống đầy mê hoặc này và những bí mật làm nên sự chênh lệch về giá trị của một cây cổ cầm này nhé

Do có nhiều người Việt Nam mong muốn học đàn, mà không có chỗ học, nên Feng xây dựng kênh này nhằm đưa đến cho các bạn thêm một nguồn tài liệu tham khảo để học tập tốt hơn

title: 古琴名曲: 俞伯荪《墨子悲丝》/ Traditional Chinese Music:  Guqin “Mozi Laments Silk”: YU Bo Sun
description:
琴曲《墨子悲丝》，又名《墨子悲歌》、《墨子》、《悲丝》等，相传为春秋战国时期鲁国墨翟所作。曲意深刻，音韵悲怆，音调悠扬，意切而情悲，旋律慷慨，墨子在这里流露出一种洁己自爱的感慨之情。此曲为广陵琴派四大操之一，曲谱最早见于明末杨抡所撰《伯牙心法》（1609年），《五知斋琴谱》、《自远堂琴谱》等也收有此曲。

春秋战国，诸侯纷争，弱肉强食，以暴凌寡，百姓疾苦不堪。墨子见染丝者而叹曰：“染于苍则苍，染于黄则黄。五入为五色，不可不慎也。非独染丝，治国亦然。”有一回，墨子见人染丝，感慨于「（丝）染了青颜料就变成青色，染了黄颜料就变成黄色。染料不同，丝的颜色也跟著变化。」联想人生何尝不是如此，而国家随人事而兴衰，社会随风俗而浮沉……于是，引发了他对社会人生的深沉思考。

乐曲反映了墨子感伤世人随波逐流、随欲浮沉而不能自拔，犹如洁丝染色，失去本来面目。

《蕉庵琴谱》题解：按此曲乃梁惠王时人墨翟所作也。翟出行，见素丝，而悲曰：人湛然同於圣体，为习俗所移。盖犹丝体本洁也，为五色所染，本同末异，清浊悬殊，人不可不慎也。审音君子能因乐而绎其旨，以墨子之悲自悲，则思所以置身於清白者，自不能已矣。宁独听其节奏之音哉？』是如“正人君子，觚稜难近”。

《五知斋琴谱》中该曲的『後记』：『《墨子》之奇，其节奏出乎意外，乃浙与中州之派也。然熟派亦尊之久矣。抚弦者当知曲中之抑扬起伏，实有敲金戛玉之声，枕石漱流之致。而一种凄然感慨，洁己自爱之怀，隐隐吐出，传之指下，诚为旷世之奇调。但必用熟派作主加以金陵之顿挫，蜀之险音，吴之含蓄，中浙之绸缪，则尽得其旨而众妙归焉矣。馀从髫年习之，亦未尽其秘奥，今参考揣摩，呕尽心血，方为全美，以俟後之君子，庶几有可采择耳。』这里仅琴派，便提到浙、中州、熟、金陵、蜀、吴等。显然，同一琴曲各派是有区别的，而琴谱编撰人主张一首琴曲可以广参各派以求『全美』的观点，也是很鲜明的。编撰者在这里既承认不同流派的客观存在，又强调了各流派间相互借鉴的必要性。另外，也可看出编撰者於此曲之用心，是『参考揣摩，呕尽心血』，认为已臻『全美』，是琴曲中较成熟者。


俞伯荪（1922—2013），男，中国四川成都人，古琴演奏家，中国非物质文化遗产项目蜀派古琴代表性传承人， “中国民乐艺术终身贡献奖”获得者。兼善扬琴、二胡、三弦、笛、箫等多种乐器。12岁进东方戏剧学校学习川剧，14岁时始学琴于陈蕴儒、龙琴舫。中年时遍访海内琴家查阜西、吴景略、溥雪斋、桂柏铸等得传琴曲及博采众长。1942年与他父亲国画家、教育家俞梓丹共同创办“古风国乐社”1948年在重庆与国画大师齐白石琴、画雅集一堂。

俞伯荪善弹《墨子悲丝》《佩兰》《高山》《流水》等曲，琴风峻急奔放、跌宕铿锵、稳健朴实。1954年—1956年，中国音乐家协会和民族音乐研究所主导，前辈古琴家查阜西、许建、王迪组成古琴普查小组，对俞伯荪采访并收录蜀派“平沙落雁”等曲入《古琴曲集》文献。1990年，中国唱片总公司成都公司录制出版发行俞伯荪《蜀派古琴》个人专辑盒带。1995年，香港雨果唱片公司录制出版发行俞伯荪《蜀中琴韵（二）CD专辑。2006年中国唱片总公司录制《蜀中琴韵》CD 专辑，《蜀中琴韵》DVD音像专辑。

背景图片（原图）: 华三川
https://huaban.com/pins/5579913992

华三川（1930年－2004年），浙江镇海人，现当代杰出的工笔人物画家。
曾担任中国美协会员、上海美术家协会理事、上海少年儿童出版社专业画家、上海市文史研究馆馆员。在他的60年艺术生涯中，勤奋耕耘，勇攀高峰，在继承传统的基础上，进行变革和创新，形成了自己独特的艺术风格，在中国工笔人物画领域里独树一帜，引人注目。已有《锦瑟年华》，《华三川人物画集》，《华三川人物线描画搞》，《华三川绘新百美图》，《华三川人物新作选》等十多部大型画集出版发行。

“Guqin” é um instrumento tradicional chinês datado de mais de três mil anos. Ele possui sete cordas e pertence a família da cítara. Tem um som suave e muito agradável. Neste vídeo a Instrumentista Bai Wuliang executa uma versão instrumental da música "Upward to to the moon". Uma linda melodia que deve ter servido de inspiração para a música " Um pedido" do músico e compositor de Hip Hop Hungria. title: Dewdrop on Bamboo Leaf 🎋 Pure Ancient Chinese Music - Healing Guqin & Bamboo Flute for Inner Peace
description:
Welcome back to the Guqin & Chill. 🌧️✨
Dewdrop on Bamboo Leaf 🌧️
Close your eyes. You are standing alone in an ancient bamboo garden
at the precise moment between night and dawn. Fine silver rain falls
in silence. Every bamboo leaf bears a single perfect dewdrop — fragile,
luminous, trembling at the edge of falling.
A single guqin note rises from total silence, its harmonic tail
dissolving into the soft sound of rain on a still garden pond and
wind breathing through the bamboo grove. A solo bamboo flute enters
with a pentatonic melody of extraordinary delicacy — each note as
fleeting as a dewdrop catching first light, each phrase followed
by deep intentional silence. One distant temple bell strikes once,
its tone spreading outward like ripples on still water, then
dissolving completely into the bamboo world.
This is music of supreme fragility. A meditation on the fleeting
perfection of a single moment before it passes.
⛔ ZERO percussion. ZERO drums. ZERO beat. ZERO western harmony.
✅ 100% pure acoustic traditional Chinese instrumentation.
✅ Seamless infinite loop — perfect for all-night meditation.
✅ 100% original composition — no sampling, no plagiarism.

description:
Welcome to Zen Asian Melodies 🌿 – Your peaceful sanctuary away from the noise of the world.

Step into a deeply reflective space where time stands still. Like a cup of cold tea in an abandoned pavilion or raindrops falling gently on withered lotus leaves, this profound instrumental beautifully captures the essence of impermanence. The resonant, lingering notes of the Guqin and Guzheng (ancient zithers) blend seamlessly with the melancholic, breathy sighs of the Xiao and Dizi (bamboo flutes). Together, they create a meditative atmosphere that washes away worldly thoughts and purifies the soul.

Life is like an ancient chessboard; victories, defeats, and worldly desires eventually fade into the mist. Let this ancient Zen music guide you to let go of your ego, release your heavy thoughts, and find comfort in absolute stillness.

title: 🍃“Lament of the Qin Palace”MiaoJiang Guzheng & Guqin 《秦宫怨》姜淼 古筝与古琴的千年悲歌
description:
Lament of the Qin Palace is a concerto for guzheng and guqin by Wang Tianyi. Set against the turmoil of the late Qin dynasty, the work uses the dialogue between two ancient instruments to express historical sorrow and the people’s longing for light. Its sound is restrained, profound, and haunting.

《秦宫怨》是王天一先生创作的古筝与古琴协奏曲。作品以秦末社会动荡为背景，通过古筝与古琴的对话，展现深沉的历史哀思与民众对光明的向往。乐曲古韵悠远，情感含蓄而深切。

《秦宮怨》は、王天一氏による古筝と古琴の協奏作品です。秦末の社会的混乱を背景に、古筝と古琴の対話を通して、深い歴史的哀感と人々の希望への憧れを表現しています。古雅で余韻の深い作品です。

title: 臺灣古琴〈流水〉蘇家俊 打譜 彈奏 Taiwan guqin
description:
版本:明嘉靖初年《西麓堂琴統》李允中轉抄本 蘇家俊打譜

說明：
宮調，十段
一、長溪舒練
二、幽磵鳴琴
三、導脈靈長
四、延流觱沸
五、雲浮拍拍
六、風起潾潾
七、望斗乘槎
八、衝烟搖擢
九、丹扃湛若
十、靈府淵然

後記：

髙山流水，蓋祖史傳伯牙鍾期事，初本一曲，唐人始二之，宋又列段，大抵皆叢山水之趣，操縵者以意逆之可也。

案：
〈流水〉一曲，琴人並不陌生。今人多彈管平湖彈奏本、衛仲樂《琴硯齋藏譜》本或顧梅羹《百瓶齋琴譜》本，三種均為百年以來流行譜本。

發掘傳統，見姚丙炎打譜明洪熙己年（1425）《神奇秘譜‧流水》，今筆者打譜《西麓堂琴統‧流水》為明 嘉靖四年(1525)已經與前者不盡相同。

據個人演奏需求及認知，對譜內作出以下改動
1.第一段第四行「大六七，挑，六」改為「大六上，挑，六」
2.第五段第三行「人三，摘，三」改為「人一，勾，三」
3.第七段第五行「蠲四」刪除，改第六行「勾五」為「蠲五」，原譜亦可。

謹以本曲向程師祝壽

用琴：靈機式古琴   歸源（香江蘇氏製）
2024年夏 於臺北三笑琴室完成打譜

title: RELAXING GUQIN MUSIC | Ancient Chinese Qin | Stress Relief | Meditation, Study, and Sleep
description:
The guqin ([kùtɕʰǐn] (listen); Chinese: 古琴) is a plucked seven-string Chinese musical instrument. It has been played since ancient times, and has traditionally been favoured by scholars and literati as an instrument of great subtlety and refinement, as highlighted by the quote "a gentleman does not part with his qin or se without good reason,"[1] as well as being associated with the ancient Chinese philosopher Confucius. It is sometimes referred to by the Chinese as "the father of Chinese music" or "the instrument of the sages". The guqin is not to be confused with the guzheng, another Chinese long stringed instrument also without frets, but with moveable bridges under each string.
There are more than 3,360 known surviving pieces of guqin music from ancient and imperial periods. On 7 November 2003, UNESCO announced that the Chinese guqin was selected as an Intangible World Cultural Heritage. In 2006, guqin was listed in the List of National Non-material Cultural Heritage in China. In 2010, a Song period guqin was sold for $22 million, making it the most expensive musical instrument ever sold. (wikipedia).

title: 古琴在巴黎 Guqin à Paris | 跨越山海 共奏一曲
description:
Une ancienne musique venue de Chine résonne aujourd’hui à Paris.
Depuis huit ans, l’Association Songlai transmet l’art du guqin au cœur de la capitale française.
Des personnes venues d’horizons différents se rencontrent à travers une même musique.

Des langues différentes, une même émotion.

Que les sonorités du guqin franchissent les frontières et rapprochent les êtres.

—

一张来自中国的古琴，在巴黎的风里，终于有了回响。

八年来，松籁·巴黎古琴协会持续在法国传习古琴艺术。

我们说着不同的语言，却拥有相同的心绪。

愿琴声跨越山海与国界，让相遇胜过疏离，让对话超越隔阂。

—

An ancient sound from China now resonates in Paris.

For the past eight years, Songlai has been sharing the art of the guqin in the heart of France.

People from different backgrounds come together through the same music.

Different languages, one shared emotion.

May the sound of the guqin transcend borders and bring people closer.

title: Jiu Kuang (酒狂) - A Scholar's Drunken Madness | Famous Guqin Masterpiece by Ruan Ji
description:
Jiu Kuang (酒狂). "Drunken Madness". This is not a song about drunkenness, but a song of protest.

This masterpiece is attributed to the great poet Ruan Ji (阮籍), one of the Seven Sages of the Bamboo Grove from the Three Kingdoms period. In a time of political turmoil, Ruan Ji feigned madness and alcoholism to escape the corrupt court, expressing his untamed spirit through music.

Listen for the staggered, almost chaotic rhythms that mimic the unsteady steps of a drunkard, yet beneath it all lies the sharp, sober clarity of a rebellious scholar. It is a controlled chaos, a powerful expression of freedom.

---

description:
In the late 7th century, a soldier named Zhang Ruoxu gained fame for his poetry, admired for its innovative style and vivid imagery. Among his works, one poem stands out and is often hailed as "the poem of poems." Written in seven-syllable lines, it opens with a serene depiction of the Yangtze River under the moonlight on a spring evening. Gradually, it transitions into a poignant reflection on the fleeting nature of human life and the heartache of separation from loved ones. Over the centuries, this masterpiece has inspired countless creations, including a guqin composition, performed beautifully by Li Zhan in this video.

title: Definición de 'Guqin': Mística musical china
description:
¿Sabías que el 'Guqin' es mucho más que un instrumento? Descubre su historia y simbolismo en la cultura china. ¿Te gustaría escuchar su sonido?

DEFINICIÓN:
El Guqin (古琴), cuyo nombre se traduce literalmente como "antiguo instrumento de cuerda", es un instrumento musical tradicional chino de siete cuerdas pulsadas. Es uno de los instrumentos más antiguos de China, con una historia documentada de más de 3,000 años.

title: #적벽 #삼국지 #고금 #구친 #칠현금 #古琴 #guqin
description:
적벽대전의 웅장한 전투 한가운데, 느닷없이 등장하는 고즈넉한 현악기 소리… 🎼
이게 바로 중국의 전통 악기 **古琴(고금, Guqin)**입니다.

겉보기엔 거문고나 가야금 비슷해 보이지만, 사실 7줄밖에 없는 3천 년 역사의 악기!
중국에서는 ‘선비의 악기’라 불렸고, 심지어 공자도 이 소리로 마음을 다스렸다고 해요.

재밌는 건,
고금 연주는 소리만 내는 게 아니라 *소리를 내지 않는 여운(여백)*마저도 음악으로 여긴다는 것.
그러니까 “침묵까지 연주한다”는 거죠. 🎵🤫

영화 적벽에서 고금 연주 장면이 괜히 들어간 게 아니에요.
칼과 창이 부딪히는 전쟁 속에서도, 음악은 사람의 마음을 움직이는 또 다른 무기였으니까요.

title: 臺灣古琴 〈正器吟〉蘇家俊 打譜 Taiwan guqin
description:
版本:明嘉靖年 《杏莊太音續譜》 蘇家俊打譜
題解：
杏莊老人曰：「漢去古未遠，古之律，度量權衡猶在。當王莽時，猶未變于古。自董卓之亂，樂律散亡，杜夔、荀勖之律，亦未合古。後周得玉斗，以為正器而生律，則近古矣。」

案：
查《隋書‧律曆志‧嘉量》記有「後周武帝保定元年辛巳五月，晉國造倉，獲古玉斗。暨五年乙酉冬十月，詔改制銅律度，遂致中和。累黍積龠，同茲玉量，與衡度無差。準為銅升，用頒天下......」
杏莊老人未能得見新莽「嘉量」，國之重寶今藏國立故宮博物院。

西漢學者劉歆（？—23年）根據《周禮》考訂，於王莽始建國元年（9）製成，用作郡國各地量測五穀的單位基準。「嘉量」上「斛」，下「斗」，左耳「升」，右耳上部「合」及下部「龠」共五種量器，換算關係為一斛十斗，一斗十升，一升十合，一合二龠，基本體現十進位的度量觀念。
器物上銘文說明尺寸、容量數值、計算方法及新莽建國歷史。

用琴：宋琴
2023年秋 於臺北三笑琴室完成打譜

title: Ancient Chinese Music: Transient Existence (Echoing Guqin & Dizi)
description:
Welcome to Zen Asian Melodies 🌿 – A tranquil space to reflect on the vast universe and find peace in our transient existence.

Imagine standing by a boundless, misty autumn river, watching a lone bird glide silently across the immense sky. This deeply atmospheric ancient instrumental captures the profound realization of how small we are in the grand scheme of the universe. The echoing, majestic tones of the Guqin (ancient zither) blend beautifully with the airy, drifting notes of the Dizi (bamboo flute), creating a vast soundscape of calm melancholy and deep peace.

Let these ancient Zen melodies remind you that life is fleeting, just like a bird crossing the autumn sky. Release your heavy burdens, let go of worldly attachments, and find comfort in the beautiful, transient nature of our existence. Stop grasping at the illusion of permanence and allow your soul to drift freely in the vastness of the universe.

✨ This profound melody is perfectly crafted for:
• Deep Zen Meditation & Soul Reflection
• Embracing the Transient Nature of Life
• Calming an Overthinking Mind in the Vast Universe

title: 《忆故人》Guqin Traditional Piece｜Ancient Chinese Music – Remembrance of Old Friends
description:
“空山寂寂，明月窗前，思君不见，忧思何限” 古琴曲《忆故人》，又名《山中思故人》，是近代广为流传的经典名曲。其乐谱最早见于清代，后经彭祉卿整理、查阜西演奏推广而成现今版本。乐曲属商调，音色天然苍凉，深情缠绵，被誉为琴乐抒情的典范。 全曲结构严谨，以空灵泛音开场，营造出空山静夜的静谧氛围。核心主题旋律婉转优美，通过多次变奏及“吟猱绰注”等技法层层推进，将思念之情从幽静怀想逐渐深化为心潮澎湃的高潮，最终复归于悠远绵的余韵，感人至深。把这个翻一下

Guqin: 南一先生
Recording / Video: 南一先生的先生

If you enjoy ancient Chinese music, guqin performances, or meditative instrumental soundscapes, welcome to subscribe for more traditional repertoire.“Within the empty mountains, silence prevails.
The bright moon hangs before the window.
I think of you, yet you cannot be seen—how boundless this sorrow becomes.”

Remembrance of Old Friends (《忆故人》), also known as Thinking of a Friend in the Mountains (《山中思故人》), is a classic guqin piece widely circulated in modern times. Its earliest surviving notation appears in the Qing Dynasty. The version commonly performed today was later compiled by Peng Zhihuang and promoted through the interpretation of master Zha Fuxi.

The piece is set in the shang mode, characterized by a naturally desolate yet deeply expressive tone, and is regarded as a model of lyrical expression in guqin music.

The composition is carefully structured. It begins with ethereal harmonics, evoking the stillness of an empty mountain night. The central theme unfolds with graceful and flowing melodic lines, gradually deepened through variations and traditional guqin techniques such as yin, nao, chuo, and zhu (vibrato, slides, and ornamental inflections).

The emotion evolves from quiet contemplation into an intense wave of longing, before finally returning to a distant and lingering resonance—leaving a profound and touching aftertaste.“空山寂寂，明月窗前，思君不见，忧思何限” 古琴曲《忆故人》，又名《山中思故人》，是近代广为流传的经典名曲。其乐谱最早见于清代，后经彭祉卿整理、查阜西演奏推广而成现今版本。乐曲属商调，音色天然苍凉，深情缠绵，被誉为琴乐抒情的典范。 全曲结构严谨，以空灵泛音开场，营造出空山静夜的静谧氛围。核心主题旋律婉转优美，通过多次变奏及“吟猱绰注”等技法层层推进，将思念之情从幽静怀想逐渐深化为心潮澎湃的高潮，最终复归于悠远绵的余韵，感人至深。把这个翻一下

title: Bài 1 - Tự Học Cổ Cầm - Giới thiệu về cổ cầm (guqin) | Fêng Channel
description:
Chào mừng các bạn đến với kênh Fêng Channel trong video  ngày hôm nay chúng ta hãy cùng đến với: Bài 1 - Tự Học Cổ Cầm - Giới thiệu về cổ cầm (guqin) Lesson 1: Basic knowledge of Guqin

Theo truyền thuyết từ xa xưa, cây cổ cầm đầu tiên có 5 dây. Nó đã xuất hiện từ rất lâu, từ thời Xuân Thu Chiến Quốc (Ngũ quốc tương vương) người ta đã biết đến cổ cầm. Vào thời vua Ôn và Ngô nhà Chu, cây cổ cầm đã được thay đổi từ 5 dây thành 7 dây. Tên gọi của nó là Guqin. Bạn có thể thấy âm thanh của Guqin rất phổ biến ở đất nước chúng ta. Khoảng âm của Guqin là 4 quãng tám, nên âm vực của nó rất rộng. Âm thanh trầm bổng của Guqin đã trở thành một phần không thể thiếu trong nền văn hóa âm nhạc truyền thống. Sự phát triển và cải tiến của Guqin qua các thời kỳ đã góp phần làm phong phú thêm di sản văn hóa của chúng ta.

Cổ Cầm dài 4 thước 5 tấc (tương ứng khoảng 1,2m), vừa khớp với con số 365 ngày, cũng đúng với thời gian Trái Đất quay quanh Mặt Trời trong một năm. Cổ Cầm có 13 chủy (chủy – một trong 5 âm thời cổ đại), đối ứng với một năm 12 tháng, thêm tháng nhuận, tổng cộng là 13. Cổ Cầm mới đầu có 5 dây đàn, đối ứng với ngũ hành,  kim mộc thủy hỏa thổ theo truyền thuyết, Văn Vương vì tưởng niệm Bá Ấp Khảo nên đã thêm một dây nữa, nên gọi là Văn Huyền.

Do có nhiều người Việt Nam mong muốn học đàn, mà không có chỗ học, nên Feng xây dựng kênh này nhằm đưa đến cho các bạn thêm một nguồn tài liệu tham khảo để học tập tốt hơn

title: The Past and Present of Guqin古琴的前世今生
description:
古琴，又称瑶琴、七弦琴，是中国最古老的弹拨乐器，距今已有三千余年历史，2003 年入选人类非物质文化遗产。琴身面板取桐木、底板用梓木，分泛音、散音、按音三种音色，对应天地人三才。琴声清雅悠远，古人常以琴修身养心，承载着淡泊平和的东方哲思，经典曲目有《流水》《良宵引》等。

The guqin, also known as the seven-stringed zither, is China’s oldest plucked instrument with a history of over 3,000 years. It was inscribed on the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage List in 2003. Its soundboard is paulownia wood and backboard catalpa wood. It produces three tones: harmonics for heaven, open tones for earth, and pressed notes for humanity. Its elegant, distant sound cultivates inner peace, embodying oriental philosophy. Famous pieces include Flowing Water and Song of a Peaceful Night.

古琴（七弦琴・瑶琴）は中国最古の撥弦楽器で、3000 年以上の歴史を持ち、2003 年に人類無形文化遺産に登録されました。表板は桐、裏板は梓で作られ、天を象徴する倍音、地を表す開放音、人にあたる押弦音の三つの音色があります。澄んで遠く響く音色は心を落ち着かせ、東洋の静かな思想を宿しており、『流水』『良宵引』などの名曲が伝わっています。


山山老师在南京成立归澐山房古琴馆，传播古琴文化，分享古琴音乐及各种中国古代传统文化艺术。欢迎关注，交流！欢迎开启小铃铛订阅我的频道！

Teacher Shanshan founded Guiyun Shanfang Guqin Studio in Nanjing, dedicated to promoting guqin culture and sharing guqin music as well as diverse traditional Chinese arts from ancient times. Feel free to follow and communicate with me!
Hit the bell and subscribe to my channel!

山山先生は南京に帰澐山房古琴館を開設し、古琴文化を広め、古琴の音楽や多種多様な中国古来の伝統芸術を発信しています。ぜひフォローして、交流しましょう！ベルマークをオンにしてチャンネル登録お願いします！

title: Yue Lee - Flowing Water (Liu Shui) – Guqin Solo/李悦 - 流水
description:
《流水》
  源自古曲《高山流水》，相传春秋琴师伯牙所作；唐代分为《高山》《流水》两支独立琴曲，最早收入于明代朱权《神奇秘谱》（1425 年）。中国十大古曲之首，古琴蜀派代表作；既是文人修身琴曲，也是走向世界最具辨识度的东方古典音乐，是人类非物质文化遗产古琴艺术的标志性曲目。
  乐曲不止摹写山水，更承载两层哲思：儒家 “智者乐水”，以流水喻君子通达包容、生生不息的品格；寄托寻觅知己、灵魂共鸣的文人情怀，是中国 “天人合一” 美学的典范。
Derived from the ancient guqin suite High Mountains and Flowing Water, traditionally attributed to Yu Boya, a master guqin player of the Spring and Autumn Period. The piece split into two independent works High Mountain and Flowing Water in the Tang Dynasty.
Earliest surviving tablature: Shen Qi Mi Pu (Magical Secret Notation) compiled by Zhu Quan in 1425, Ming Dynasty.
Far more than a landscape sketch of rivers, the melody carries dual profound meanings:
Confucian philosophy: “The wise delight in water”, using flowing water as a metaphor for the virtuous scholar’s open-mindedness and eternal vitality.
Literati longing for soulmates and spiritual communion, embodying China’s core aesthetic of harmony between human and nature.
Ranked top among China’s Ten Great Ancient Melodies and the signature work of Sichuan Guqin School. As the most internationally recognizable classical Chinese instrumental piece, it stands as an iconic treasure of Guqin, inscribed on UNESCO’s Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

title: Yue Lee - A Laugh by the Sea (Guqin Version)/李悦 - 沧海一声笑
description:
《沧海一声笑》（古琴改编版）
作曲：黄霑
原作是华语武侠音乐里程碑；古琴改编版打通传统古琴与大众武侠文化，兼具文人琴的清雅与江湖曲的旷达，是海内外古琴雅集、民乐短视频最热门演奏曲目之一。
Original Composer & Lyricist: James Wong (Wong Jum)
The original song is a milestone of Chinese martial arts soundtracks. Its guqin adaptation bridges classical literati guqin culture and popular wuxia aesthetics. It is a staple repertoire for guqin recitals, gatherings and Chinese instrumental short-video content at home and abroad.

title: 臺灣古琴〈高山〉蘇家俊 打譜 彈奏 Taiwan guqin
description:
版本:明嘉靖初年《西麓堂琴統》李允中轉抄本 蘇家俊打譜

說明：
宮調，八段
一﹕嵩嶽崢嶸
二﹕崑崙巀嶪
三﹕天空獨嶂
四﹕霞起群峰
五﹕晴嵐積翠
六﹕暮煙凝紫
七﹕躡屩思登
八﹕振衣懷嘯

曲解見於〈流水〉後：髙山流水，蓋祖史傳伯牙鍾期事，初本一曲，唐人始二之，宋又列段，大抵皆叢山水之趣，操縵者以意逆之可也。


後記：
〈高山〉，近世主流是新浙派所打譜的清乾隆年《春草堂琴譜‧高山》，琴家鄭云飛所奏更是經典。明嘉靖初年《西麓堂琴統‧高山》異於該譜，與較早的《神奇秘譜》接近，後者不分段。整曲清新自然，有竹杖芒鞋輕勝馬之感。情景確是身在此山中。
照片為三笑所攝，是日本青森岩木山，有「津輕富士」美譽，氣象令人溫暖。

謹以本曲向恩師楊朝証祝壽

用琴：落霞式古琴   入雲月（香江蘇氏製）
2025年春 於臺北三笑琴室完成打譜

title: 廖艾斯   古琴｜平沙落雁《梅庵琴譜》 ｜Guqin－ ping sha luo yan
description:
平沙落雁—此次演奏版本為《梅庵琴派》

此曲流傳相當廣泛，也是琴曲中版本最多的樂曲，可見「雁」是多麼受到琴人所喜愛。《梅庵琴譜》：「本曲至曲終時才是落雁。全曲皆是雁群由起飛而迴旋於空際及上下翻騰飛嗚之狀。起首狀雁群分批起飛，逐漸迴旋之意。第一段末有少停息之意。第二段繼續起飛，以大分開狀復翔時盤旋之狀，繼以徇序飛行。第三段之意相仿，兼有拍翼之聲。第四段末表示在沙灘上低翔之後伏於沙灘之狀。第五段仍是低翔。第六段是上入雲霄，鳴聲嘈雜，及急嗚之後漸次下翔之狀。第七段是雁群以翼拍沙之後又復掠地面低徊，而以尾聲狀群雁分批下落於沙灘之上而終結。第六段為諸譜所無，乃諸城琴宗所獨有，而燕卿先生又復加工，在梅庵授琴時尚一再修改，可以證之。」

title: Louisaevivo — Endless Abyss | from Dreaming of Lanting | Experimental Guqin × AI Electronic MV
description:
This experimental music video takes the timbre of the guqin as its acoustic origin. The work begins with samples of the guqin’s subtle sonic textures, captured through different performance techniques, and transforms them through AI models into new electronic timbres that hover between the real and the virtual.

After composing these AI-generated sounds with other electronic elements, the artist introduces further layers of AI-driven noise processing, creating a soundscape where ancient resonance meets post-human electronic texture.

The work explores the fusion of ancient Chinese musical philosophy, artificial intelligence, and futuristic electronic music.

description: 当雨滴落在屋顶时，整个世界似乎都变得宁静了。在这样的雨天，您是否也想找一首舒缓的音乐来伴您度过？今天，我为您带来了一首美丽的古琴曲，由GUZHENG、笛子和GUQIN共同演奏。这种音乐不仅可以帮助您放松，还能带您回到中国传统音乐的世界。随着美丽的旋律，您可以感受到中国文化的深厚底蕴。所以，请您静下心来，细细品味这首音乐。如果您喜欢这首音乐，请不要忘记点赞和订阅我的频道，以便您可以及时观看我发布的最新视频。同时，如果您有朋友也喜欢这种音乐，请分享给他们，我相信他们也会非常喜欢。

title: Handcrafting The Ancient Guqin With Precision Wood Carving Techniques
description:
Handcrafting The Ancient Guqin With Precision Wood Carving Techniques

A master carves the soundbox of a traditional Guqin. He uses a chisel to hollow the solid wood. Each stroke follows ancient patterns. This is the soul of Chinese music. The shavings show incredible skill.
This instrument has three thousand years of history. Specific wood types ensure perfect resonance. Crafting one takes years. The internal shape defines its unique voice. It is a work of art.

Keywords: Guqin Craftsmanship, Traditional Chinese Instrument, Wood Carving, Ancient Music, Handcrafted Zither, Chinese Culture, Master Woodworker, Acoustic Engineering, Artisan Skills, Musical Instrument Making

title: Quiet Your Mind With Ancient Guqin | 432 Hz Sleep Focus Music
description:
A 33-minute ancient guqin soundscape for quieting a restless mind, sleep preparation, reading, and low-distraction evening focus.

This MindfulHz session blends the soft plucked resonance of the Chinese guqin with a gentle 432 Hz-inspired calm atmosphere, warm room tone, and cinematic night-study visuals. Use it as a quiet background when you want fewer words, less noise, and a steady musical anchor for settling down.

432 Hz is used here as a creative musical tuning association, not as a medical treatment or guaranteed health effect. Keep the volume comfortable, breathe slowly, and let the guqin become a calm point of attention.

Chapters
00:00 - Quiet mind begins
03:00 - Ancient guqin stillness
06:00 - Soft evening focus
10:00 - Low-distraction calm
15:00 - Reading and sleep preparation
20:00 - Warm night study room
25:00 - Gentle plucked resonance
30:00 - Quiet closing stillness

Best for
- Sleep preparation music
- Quiet mind meditation
- Reading and writing background
- Low-distraction focus
- Ancient instrument ambience
- 432 Hz calm music

Cultural respect note: The guqin is an ancient Chinese seven-string zither associated with scholar music, refined listening, and meditative stillness. This MindfulHz piece is offered as a contemporary ambient sleep and focus soundscape with respect for that tradition.

AI disclosure: Visuals and production elements may use AI-assisted tools. The soundscape, pacing, and final presentation are curated for MindfulHz with human creative direction.

Subscribe for calming world-instrument meditations built around emotional use cases, distinctive instruments, and cinematic stillness.

title: 放鬆身心，安心入眠 🌙 Relaxing Guqin Music for Deep Sleep｜Stress Relief & Calm Mind
description:
🌿 歡迎來到 Relaxing Guqin Music｜清心古琴音樂

竹林夜靜，安心入眠 🌙 Guqin Sleep Music for Deep Sleep｜Relaxing Chinese Zen Music

在安靜的竹林夜色中，微光穿過霧氣，古老的小徑與溪水慢慢沉入寧靜。溫柔悠遠的古琴聲，像一陣輕柔的晚風，陪伴您放下疲憊，放慢呼吸，讓身心慢慢回到平靜。

這是一段適合深度睡眠、睡前放鬆、舒緩壓力、冥想靜心、閱讀休息與放空思緒的古琴音樂。願竹林、夜色、流水與琴音，陪您安心入眠，進入安穩而深沉的休息時光。

Welcome to Relaxing Guqin Music 🌙
This Guqin sleep music is inspired by a quiet bamboo forest at night, soft mist, flowing water, ancient Chinese scenery, and a peaceful Zen atmosphere.

Created for deep sleep, relaxation, stress relief, meditation, reading, healing, and inner peace, this video is perfect for anyone who wants to calm the mind, relax the body, and drift into a peaceful night.

Let the soothing sound of Guqin, bamboo forest ambience, gentle water, and relaxing Chinese Zen music guide you into deep relaxation and restful sleep.

🎶 Guqin Sleep Music｜Deep Sleep Music｜Relaxing Chinese Zen Music｜Bamboo Forest Music｜Sleep Music｜Meditation Music｜Stress Relief｜Calm Mind｜Inner Peace

🏮 訂閱 Relaxing Guqin Music，讓古琴、竹林與夜色，陪伴您走向安眠、平靜與深度放鬆。

title: Chinese Guqin  : Flowing water Guqin 流水 古琴曲
description:
Guqin song "running water" has a long history. In the Ming Dynasty Zhu Quan's "magic secret manual", the caption of "high mountain" and "running water" recorded that "high mountain" and "running water" had only one song. At the beginning, ambition cared about the high mountain and said that benevolent people liked the mountain. Later, ambition cared about the running water and said that wise people liked the water. In the Tang Dynasty, it was divided into two songs, with no number of segments. In the Song Dynasty, it was divided into four segments of "high mountain" and eight segments of "running water". Since then, there have been more than 30 kinds of musical scores published in running water. Their basic content and spirit come down in one continuous line, and they basically maintain the structure of 8 paragraphs. During the period from Xianfeng to Guangxu of the Qing Dynasty, Zhang Kongshan, a Taoist from Qingcheng, a Sichuan pianist, added a paragraph between the original fifth and sixth paragraphs to become the ninth paragraph

In the first paragraph, the whole song starts to flow, which vaguely implies the theme tone of the whole song. 2、 The third paragraph uses overtone to write all kinds of spring sounds of mountains, streams and waterfalls. 4、 The fifth section shows that the spring of thousands of gullies flows out of the mountain and into the flood, and gradually has a turbulent trend. Since the sixth section, the water flows into the vast ocean, and the rapids cross the gorge and beach, forming a momentum of rough waves and surging unstoppable, conveying the character of fearing difficulties and dangers and forging ahead bravely. 7、 The eighth segment is the afterwave after the climax, which is slow and urgent, released and closed from time to time, and gradually calmed down. The ninth paragraph is the finale of the passing weather. This piano song is full of the harmony between man and nature, and exudes the atmosphere of the harmony of nature, earth and people. This song has both lyricism and simulation, the combination of virtual and real, the integration of scenes and the lofty atmosphere.

On August 20, 1977, the Voyager spacecraft launched by NASA into outer space carried a gold-plated record that is said to have been preserved for 1 billion years, including the song "running water" played by Mr. Guan Pinghu, a famous pianist in China.
古琴曲《流水》历史悠久。明代朱权《神奇秘谱》中《高山》、《流水》的题解记载：“《高山》、《流水》二曲本只一曲。初，志在乎高山，言仁者乐山之意。后，志在乎流水，言智者乐水之意。至唐分为两曲，不分段数，至宋分《高山》为四段，《流水》为八段”。此后刊载《流水》的琴谱多达30多种，它们的基本内容和精神一脉相承，也基本保持了8段的结构。清咸丰至光绪年间川派琴家青城道士张孔山在原第五、六段之间加了一段，成为9段
        首段，起全曲水流之势，隐约暗示全曲的主题音调。二、三段用泛音写出山涧小溪潺潺、瀑布飞溅的各种泉声。四、五段表现万壑之泉由细流出山汇入洪流，并渐有汹涌之势。自六段起，水流汇入浩瀚汪洋，急流穿峡过滩，形成惊涛骇浪、奔腾难挡的气势，传达了不畏艰险、勇往直前的品格。七、八段为高潮之后的余波，忽缓忽急，时放时收，渐渐平复。第九段以杳渺徐逝的气象终曲。 这首琴曲充满着人与自然的和谐之音，散发了天籁、地籁、人籁相知相合、浑然一体的气象。此曲兼有抒情性和模拟性，虚实结合、情景相融、气象高远。
        1977年8月20日，美国宇航局向外太空发射的“旅行者”号宇宙飞船上搭载了一张据称能保存10亿年之久的镀金唱片，其中就有我国著名琴家管平湖先生演奏的《流水》一曲。

description:
★ Hello People of all over The World! Welcome to my Channel!
★ I Hope everyone feel comfortable with all of music in my channel !

🎵Introduction---The  Chinese  guqin: 古琴) is a plucked seven-string Chinese musical instrument of the zither family.It has been played since ancient times, and has traditionally been favoured by scholars and literati as an instrument of great subtlety and refinement, as highlighted by the quote "a gentleman does not part with his qin or se without good reason," as well as being associated with the ancient Chinese philosopher Confucius.

 It is sometimes referred to by the Chinese as "the father of Chinese music" or "the instrument of the sages". The guqin is not to be confused with the guzheng, another Chinese long zither also without frets, but with moveable bridges under each string.

title: The Eagles - Hotel California - Reimagined on the Traditional Chinese Guzheng | Moyun
description:
This time I will bring you a fun loop form! Different from the previous multi-part ensemble, it is possible to complete a piece of music in one go, and the process is quite interesting~

The use of guitar in Hotel California can be regarded as one of the classic arrangements in history, especially the 1994 live version. So I made some deletions in the vocal part, focusing on the two guitar solos of the prelude and the ending. Hope you like it!

The costume this time is Wei Jin style wide-sleeved cross-collared Hanfu. For the convenience of plucking the instrument, panbo are added. title: Guqin- Traditional Chinese Music
description:
Guqin: Li Xiangting (Ancient Rhyme of Yuan Song) "Purple Flower"

Guqin "Purple Flower" is taken from Mr. Li Xiangting's Guqin and Xiao improvisation album "Yuanqu Ancient Rhyme".
Following "The Poetic Flavor of the Tang Dynasty" and "The Poetic Meaning of the Song Dynasty", Mr. Li Xiangting's "The Ancient Rhyme of Yuan Opera" once again interprets the unique artistic charm of Yuan Opera with the guqin and Xiao.
Either long-distance, sincere feelings, sadness and grudges, or beautiful and charming, Mr. Li Xiangting is inspired and inspiring.
 His mellow improvisations and unvoiced rhymes create a transcendence of time and space.
The background of the music is based on the Yuan Dynasty poet Wang Shifu's Yuetiao "Purple Flower Preface". Yuan man Wang Shifu used to be an official in the court, and in his later years he chose to be like Fan Li, sailing on the five lakes, free and easy, with a song "Purple Flower", telling his ideal.

title: 古琴《禅定》: 李祥霆 / Chinese Traditional Music, Guqin “Chan Ding (Buddhist Meditation)”: LI Xiang Ting
description:
古琴曲《禅定》选自李祥霆先生的专辑《心》(风华国韵系列) © 2009 龙源音乐。
“禅”是外不着相，不执着一切境界相是为禅；“定”是内不动心。潘国静禅师曰: “禅者藏也，未见此物，不知何物，只有依悟，明心见性。” 由「凡人」到「成佛」整个过程中，起关键性作用的是「禅定」，没有它发挥积极作用，「凡人」是无法达到「成佛」的境界。
独处之时，听一曲李祥霆先生的《禅定》，悠然的琴声犹如一缕佛香，让心底的一抹浮躁烟消云散。故而在纷繁世事中寻得一片安逸，静坐敛心，专注一境，思行悟禅，忘言息虑。

图片来源: 薛大庸人物画 龙牙居遁禅师《学道》
https://www.yczihua.com/goods-6110.html
薛大庸，字兰石，1966年出生于河南。中国书画家杂志社艺术总监。作品多次在东南亚及日本、韩国展出，部分作品被中国当代书画院永久收藏。

It takes at least 18 months to make a Guqin, a 7-stringed zither that dates back over 3,000 years.
The process has more than 100 steps, including crushing pearls and gemstones and mix them with raw lacquer.
The instrument has been hailed as the "father of Chinese music."

title: 古琴《面壁》：李祥霆 / Chinese Music, Guqin “Facing the Wall”: LI Xiang Ting
description:
古琴曲《面壁》选自中国古琴大师北派代表李祥霆录制的即兴佛乐发烧专辑《心》(风华国韵系列 © 2009 龙源音乐) 。

专辑中使用的传世唐朝古琴“九霄环佩”，被视为“鼎鼎唐物”，声音尽善尽美。此琴是唐玄宗第三子李亨即帝位的至德丙申年(西元756年) 宫廷作品，琴韵独特，空灵苍远，浑厚恬静，古朴幽深，行家誉为琴中“仙品”。专辑《心》是在李祥霆所理解之佛家精神所感觉之佛家气象前提下选定十个题目即兴演奏录制而成。

古琴曲《面壁》，其意至深，其理至微，其音至乐，是明心见性之正音。以正音得正悟，在证道后恢复本真的“心”，永不退转，方可得大智慧究竟圆满。

背景图片: 卢志军《红衣达摩》

description:
Immerse yourself in the tranquil sounds of traditional Chinese Guqin string instruments with this 8-hour healing music video for sleep. Specially curated to soothe your mood, heal your heart organ, and uplift your soul, this video is the perfect companion for relaxation, meditation, and self-reflection.

The Chinese Guqin, a traditional string instrument with a history spanning over 3,000 years, is known for its gentle and calming sound. Master Shen Wu's 8-hour video features a selection of the most beautiful and healing Guqin pieces, carefully chosen to help you relax, meditate, and find inner peace.

Whether you're looking to de-stress after a long day, improve your mood, or simply enjoy the beautiful sounds of the Chinese Guqin, this video is for you. Sit back, relax, and let the music take you on a journey of healing and relaxation.

Another masterpeace from my collection.

Traditional Sichuan folk music played by very famous and honourable local musician WANG Hua-de. Played by Qin (Guqin) made by LEI Wei in Tang Dynasty (618-907 A.D.)

Photos made by famous Sichuan photographer Chen Jin. Photos taken from his book "Teahouse".

"Chengdu, the capital city of Sichuan province, is really famous for traditional teahouses around the streets . If you like tea and chinese tea culture, you totally need to go to Sichuan. title: Water Tranquility 水境 中國風音樂 | Ancient Chinese Guqin & Xiao Flute | Zen Meditation & Relaxation Music
description:
霧深之處，天地無形。
音起之時，心念自止。

一人立於舟上，
不語，不動，
只是讓簫聲穿過濃霧，
落入水面與夜色之中。

此刻無需追尋、無需理解，
讓所有未說完的心事化入夜波，
讓未安定的念頭沉入霧後。

在聲音之外，
有更安靜的地方；
在呼吸之內，
有更柔軟的自己。

願你靜下來，
不求證悟，
只願安住。



When the mist grows deeper,
the world becomes softer.

When the Xiao begins,
the mind grows still.

Stand within the silence,
and let every breath
return to where it first began.

Here, nothing must be solved,
nothing must be reached.
Only stay—
and peace unveils itself.

title: 【箏鼓和鳴】權御天下 Sun Quan The Emperor (Guzheng&Drum Ver.)
description:
Godlike I go BOOOM!
Hope you enjoy this very Han Chinese styled music.
Original Song: 權御天下(洛天依) - https://youtu.be/LJms1CGHjn8

Credits:
Music: 乌龟sui
Guzheng/PV/Mixing - 墨韵随步摇
Percussion - 司鼓君
Chinese calligraphy: 愚是知
Art Director: 小甫哥
Mixing Director: 天音之旋
Costum: 汉服Hanfu - 对襟襦裙

Reprint from bilibili: http://www.bilibili.com/video/av2188740/
墨韵weibo: http://weibo.com/u/2616755905?refer_flag=1001030102_

墨韵：《权御天下》筝鼓合奏版（又名《墨韵第二号古筝练习曲》233）该来的终于来了！久等！这次的作品有着空前的合作阵容，多数是幕后的，但都为之付出了很多心血，参与拍摄的除了司鼓君都不是专业人士，还请多指教。此曲古筝部分的改编力度很大，因为我想尽量呈现一个丰富而有层次的演奏，开头致敬经典筝鼓合奏曲目——常静大神的《篆音》。感谢作者乌龟的授权，希望大家能喜欢！【UP主着装：对襟襦裙加半臂，请勿掐架】

title: Water Zen 水境古琴簫 | Ancient Chinese Guqin & Xiao Flute | Meditation & Deep Relaxation
description:
歡迎來到 Melodic Calm。
這裡的音樂源自喜馬拉雅山與東方禪修文化的靜謐啟發。
透過古琴、簫、頌缽、木魚等傳統樂器，
我們創作出結合禪意、冥想與心靈療癒的聲音，
幫助您放下煩憂，回到內心的平靜與覺察。

在聲音之中，感受寧靜、慈悲與自在的流動。

title: Steps to Make a Chinese Guqin
description:
In Shuangqiao Town, Shunqing #District, Nanchong City, there is an #ancient and #simple #farmhouse that has been steeped in the #sound of a century-old zither. Zhao Ou, a rural guqin #player and the third-generation inheritor of the intangible cultural heritage of "Zhiyun Guqin #Traditional Making Technique" in Shunqing District, Nanchong City, has hands full of marks of dialogue with wood, endowing every string and thread of the guqin with the family's traditional guqin making skills.
The process of making a guqin is meticulous and lengthy. To make a guqin, one needs to go through dozens of procedures such as material selection, grooving, and tuning. The process of making a zither is also a kind of mental training. Let's step into Shunqing District, Nanchong City, Sichuan Province together and experience a "heart-wrenching" journey.

title: The Healing Art of Guqin Making | Traditional Chinese Craft & Intangible Heritage
description:
The Healing Art of Guqin Making | Traditional Chinese Craft & Intangible Heritage

🔹 Description:
Discover the timeless beauty of the guqin, an ancient Chinese musical instrument crafted from a single piece of wood. Each step in the guqin-making process reflects patience, slowness, and meticulous attention to detail—qualities that bring peace, balance, and healing in today’s fast-paced world.

In this video, master guqin maker Lu Yuping demonstrates the artistry, cultural heritage, and spiritual significance of the guqin. By preserving this intangible cultural heritage, he keeps alive a tradition that has been cherished for centuries.

If you’re passionate about traditional Chinese culture, mindfulness, and the therapeutic power of music, this video will inspire and connect you with the deeper meaning of craftsmanship.

title: 空月｜古琴 古箏 簫 | Empty Moon | Chinese Guqin, Guzheng & Xiao Flute | Relaxation Music & Zen Meditation
description:
《空月》—— 古琴、古箏與簫在夜色下的相遇。
古琴沉靜悠遠，古箏清澈靈動，簫聲空靈如風。三者交織出一幅如水墨般的意境，彷彿在月下遙望，心隨音律漂泊於天地之間。
這首樂曲呈現中國傳統樂器的細膩之美，適合靜靜欣賞、沉澱心靈，體會「空月」的幽遠與空靈。


---

🌙
“Empty Moon” — A meeting of the Guqin, Guzheng, and Xiao flute beneath the night sky.
The Guqin carries deep stillness, the Guzheng sparkles with clarity, and the Xiao flute drifts like a whispering breeze. Together, they weave an atmosphere like ink painting under the moon, inviting the listener to wander between heaven and earth.
This piece highlights the delicate beauty of Chinese traditional instruments — a journey of elegance, tranquility, and poetic resonance.

title: 古琴名曲: 龚一《醉渔唱晚》 Traditional Chinese Music:  Guqin “A Drunken Fisherman Sings in the Evening”: GONG Yi
description:
古琴名曲《醉渔唱晚》，录自龚一先生《醉渔唱晚》教学示范。

古琴名曲《醉渔唱晚》现存多种版本。《西麓堂琴统》中记述此曲为陆龟蒙与皮日休泛舟松江，见渔父醉歌，遂写此曲。《五知斋琴谱》中认为《醉渔唱晚》为”后世隐流”所作，亦古调也。与渔歌音同而调异。此曲实有笑傲烟云、醉乡酣美之意，非尘埃奔走、粗心浮气所能领其趣也。“瓮里乾坤大，壶中日月长”聆斯曲亦当作如是解。《杏庄太音补遗》中《醉渔唱晚》解题：杏庄老人曰，此曲盖本张仲宗词云，明月太虚同一照，浮家泛宅忌昏晓，醉眼冷看朝市闹，烟波老，谁能惹得闲烦恼之意。

《琴学初津》中《醉渔唱晚》后记：原谱逸云，亭史曰奇音妙趣，描冩醉态，如闻其声，如见其人。按旧谱声调乖戾，定音不一。诚一堂定为徵音，自逺堂定作商音，定徵音者似一弦主宫之调，定商音者似三弦主宫之调，皆非是曲之本旨。兹依诚一堂本细审用法，实系太簇宫韵最为近，古因曲中不用一三六弦散音，常用三弦九半，二七弦徽外，四弦八徽，五弦九徽，各弦六半，六弦六徽等位，皆属正调二变之位，岂非太簇宫曲明矣。盖原评云，奇音妙趣者，言其摹冩醉态之神，取音之奇，如闻如见，若以为异腔怪调而赞之曰妙趣，安得不负古人制曲之精微耶。凡古之传谱皆书大畧，音节既熟於胸 ，但以指下准绳，後人未究其奥旨，而见收四弦者，辄指曰徵音也，商音也，伪以传伪无所是正，古曲雅音有往无返，恨不能执卷以追问古人良可慨也。是谱悉为整订推详，虽未必与古曲契合，聊尽夫体用之当然耳。曲系隐流所作，传者不详姓氏，啸傲烟云，诚古调也。 红梨聼松客跋

龚一，1941年出生，江苏启东人。知名古琴演奏家，古琴教育家，上海民族乐团一级演员，国家一级演员。曾任上海民族乐团团长、“今虞”琴社社长，现任中国琴会荣誉会长、上海音乐学院研究生导师、中国音乐家协会和上海音乐家协会理事。

title: The Best 10 Traditional Chinese Music of Guqin #relaxingmusic #relaxing
description:
Guqin (pronounced goo-chin) is a traditional seven-stringed plucked instrument from China, with over 3,000 years of history.
It is often referred to as the "Instrument of the Sages" due to its association with Chinese scholars, philosophers, and literati. The guqin is not only a musical instrument but also a symbol of Chinese intellectual and cultural refinement, representing harmony, balance, and introspection in traditional Chinese aesthetics. Known for its delicate, ethereal sound and profound cultural symbolism, the guqin was recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2003.

Here, I have collected ten famous guqin pieces. They are performed by renowned guqin masters and represent the finest versions I could gather.
Among them, I highly recommend the first piece, "Guangling San". This piece carries a legendary aura, telling the story of an ancient assassin seeking revenge for his father. It has long been cherished by scholars and intellectuals of ancient China who valued solitude and transcendence.
At one point, "Guangling San" was lost to history. The version we have today is a modern interpretation based on an ancient musical score.

0:00:00 "Guangling San" (Guǎnglíng Sàn)
0:07:20 "Flowing Water" (Liú Shuǐ)
0:14:55 "Mist and Clouds over the Xiao and Xiang Rivers" (Xiāo Xiāng Shuǐ Yún)
0:23:40 "Three Variations on the Plum Blossom" (Méihuā Sān Nòng)
0:31:57 "Three Refrains on the Yang Pass" (Yáng Guān Sān Dié)
0:37:25 "Drunken Fisherman Singing in the Evening" (Zuì Yú Chàng Wǎn)

0:47:08  "Eighteen Songs of a Nomad Flute" (Hújiā Shíbā Pāi)
1:03:03 "Wild Geese Descending on the Sandbank" (Píng Shā Luò Yàn)

title: 这首《桂枝香》好听到流泪Guizhixiang: Beautiful and sad tunes performed by Chinese instruments
description:
《桂枝香》是自得琴社朝代系列音乐会音乐会《琴为何物·宋·水云归》的压轴曲目，倾诉了一段繁华落尽的故国往事，令许多观众动情而落泪。

登临送目，正故国晚秋，天气初肃。千里澄江似练，翠峰如簇。归帆去棹残阳里，背西风，酒旗斜矗。彩舟云淡，星河鹭起，画图难足。
念往昔，繁华竞逐，叹门外楼头，悲恨相续。千古凭高对此，谩嗟荣辱。六朝旧事随流水，但寒烟衰草凝绿。至今商女，时时犹唱，后庭遗曲。
——《桂枝香·金陵怀古》宋·王安石

title: 人生必听名曲【古琴】《广陵散》“Guangling San”: The Most Legendary and Heroic Melody in Chinese Guqin History.
description:
广陵散
此曲写聂政刺韩王之事。弦外长虹，破韩庭昏佞。指间霹雳，醒嵇康奇梦。琴心剑胆犹在，广陵绝响今存。

神奇秘谱：臞仙按琴史曰，“晋书载：广陵散者，‘嵇康，字叔夜，谯国之人也。尝游会稽，宿华阳亭，引琴而弹；夜分，忽有客诣之，称是古人，与康共谈音律，辞致清辨，因索琴弹之，为广陵散曲，声调绝伦，遂以授康，仍誓不传人，亦不言其姓字。时司马懿为大将军，康与钟会为长史。会每与康交，而康不为之礼，会以此憾之，因谮康欲助毋丘俭。司马懿既昵信会，遂害之。康将刑东市，顾视日影，索琴弹之曰，‘昔袁孝己尝从吾学广陵散，吾每靳固之，广陵散于今绝矣’。时年四十。海内之士莫不痛之。帝寻悟而悔焉”，又琴书曰：“嵇康广陵散本四十一拍，不传于世。惟康之甥袁孝己能琴，每从康学靳惜不与，后康静夜鼓琴弹广陵散，孝己窃从户外听之。至乱声小息，康疑其有人，推琴而止，出户果见孝己。止得三十三拍。后孝己会止息意，续成八拍，共四十一拍，序引在外。世亦罕知焉。”然广陵散曲，世有二谱。今予所取者，隋宫中所收之谱。隋亡而入于唐，唐亡流落于民间者有年，至宋高宗建炎间，复入于御府。经九百三十七年矣，予以此谱为正。故取之。

《神奇秘谱》由明代宁王朱权于1425年编纂刊行，是中国现存最早、保存最为完整的古琴谱专集。该谱分三卷，共收琴曲六十四首。上卷称《太古神品》，收十六曲，如《广陵散》《酒狂》《古风操》等“太古之操，昔人不传之秘”。中、下卷称《霞外神品》，收四十八曲，如《乌夜啼》《大胡笳》《离骚》等编纂者“昔所受之曲”，多为宋元间流传较广的琴曲。其堪称历史上最具声望的传谱，足为琴人之瑰宝。

  2025年适逢《神奇秘谱》刊行六百周年，自得琴社以乐代酒，再响古韵，更翻新声，邀众知音共醉丝桐。本次音乐会精选《神奇秘谱》琴曲十三首，其中《山居吟》《樵歌》《天风环佩》《大胡笳》《广陵散》五曲为古琴独奏，听烂柯斤斤，琳琅仙声，塞外胡笳，戈矛霹雳，再响太古遗音；《玄默》《猗兰》《列子御风》《泛沧浪》《离骚》《古风操》六曲为古曲新衍，丝竹相和，慕贤追圣，可观逍遥物外之意，亦可仰上下求索之心；《酒狂》《乌夜啼》二曲为琴曲移植，擘阮擎琵，更听争巢啼乌几声，共竹林风流一醉。

  六百年琴韵，高山仰止可听今声。
  六百年琴心，流水不断更待知音。
"Guangling San" (Melody of Guangling)

This piece recounts the story of Nie Zheng’s assassination of the King of Han. The melody carries the force of a long rainbow piercing the corrupt Han court; beneath the fingers, thunder awakens Ji Kang’s extraordinary dream. The qin’s heart and the swordsman’s courage remain—the lost strains of Guangling live on today.

Shen Qi Mi Pu: Zhu Quan (“Recluse Immortal”) records: Ji Kang learned this peerless melody from a mysterious night visitor, who swore him to secrecy. Kang later offended the official Zhong Hui, who falsely accused him. Condemned to death, Kang played this tune one last time in the market, lamenting, “Now Guangling San ends with me.”

Only his nephew Yuan Xiaoji overheard part of it, later completing the piece. Of the two surviving scores, the version here comes from a Sui palace manuscript, passed down through Tang and Song courts over 937 years—the edition I deem authentic.

Compiled and published in 1425 by Zhu Quan, the Prince of Ning during the Ming Dynasty, Shen Qi Mi Pu (The Mysterious and Marvelous Tablature) stands as the earliest and most completely preserved dedicated collection of guqin tablature in existence in China. The anthology is divided into three volumes containing a total of sixty-four pieces. The first volume, entitled Tai Gu Shen Pin (Divine Pieces of Great Antiquity), includes sixteen compositions such as "Guangling San," "Jiu Kuang," and "Gu Feng Cao"—described as "secret melodies from remote antiquity, passed down by none." The middle and final volumes, collectively titled Xia Wai Shen Pin (Divine Pieces Beyond the Rosy Clouds), contain forty-eight pieces including "Wu Ye Ti," "Da Hu Jia," and "Li Sao," which the compiler noted were tunes he "had received in the past," largely comprising qin works widely circulated during the Song and Yuan dynasties. Hailed as the most prestigious transmitted tablature in history, it remains a treasured gem for all guqin practitioners.

The year 2025 marks the 600th anniversary of Shen Qi Mi Pu’s publication. To honor this milestone, Zi De Guqin Studio offers music in place of wine, reanimating ancient harmonies and reinventing their sounds, inviting all connoisseurs of the art to share in an intoxicating journey through strings and wood. This album presents live recordings from the Shen Qi Mi Pu 600th Anniversary Concert, which features thirteen pieces carefully selected from the tablature. Among them, five—"Shan Ju Yin," "Qiao Ge," "Tian Feng Huan Pei," "Da Hu Jia," and "Guang Ling San"—are performed as guqin solos, evoking echoes of timeless antiquity: the legendary axe-handle turning to dust, ethereal jade-like tones, frontier reed-pipes beyond the Great Wall, and the fierce clangor of spear and shield. Another six pieces—"Xuan Mo," "Yi Lan," "Lie Zi Yu Feng," "Fan Cang Lang," "Li Sao," and "Gu Feng Cao"—are newly arranged from ancient themes, blending silk and bamboo instruments in harmonious dialogue that honors the sages and stirs the soul; here one may sense a spirit wandering free from worldly cares, and glimpse a heart that ceaselessly seeks truth. The remaining two, "Jiu Kuang" and "Wu Ye Ti," are adapted from the original qin scores and recast for the ruan and the pipa; listen closely, and you may catch the crows squabbling over their nest, or join the unrestrained revelry of the Bamboo Grove.

Six hundred years of qin melody—behold the lofty mountain, and hear its voice in today’s strings.
Six hundred years of qin spirit—like ever‑flowing streams, it awaits the listening heart.

description:
《古琴名曲解说》：

题 解

南宋末年，蒙古骑兵席卷神州、马踏江南，中原汉家王朝有史以来第一次陷入全盘亡国的危局。奈何朝廷奸佞当道，无数志士空有报国之志。大厦将倾，文明遭劫，这对于广大的儒生阶层来说，是一种巨大的伤痛，郭沔就是其中之一。其以琴为笔，借潇湘之烟波发亡国之哀音，托九嶷之云卷寄满腔之悲愤。水云摇荡，满目凄凉；云遮九嶷，神州之殇！斯曲虽状水云之态，实乃寓家国之情尔。

title: Ancient Chinese Guqin Music 🌸
description:
Chinese guqin music is one of the oldest and most respected musical traditions in Taiwan (Republic of China), with a history of over three thousand years.

Played on the seven-stringed guqin, it has been closely associated with scholars and philosophers as a means of self-cultivation and reflection.

Known for its subtle, elegant tones and expressive techniques, guqin music conveys deep philosophical meaning and is deeply connected to Confucian, Daoist, and Buddhist thought.

Tashi Delek 🌿

title: Chinese Guqin 古琴
description:
The guqin (古琴/gǔ qín) is a plucked seven-string Chinese musical instrument of the zither family. It has been played since ancient times.

Legend has it that guqin first came into being as a long single-string zither created by Fuxi, one of the primogenitors of the Chinese civilization, who taught people means to survive and later originated “I Ching,” or “The Book of Changes.”

In the hands of Emperor Shun (2294-2184 BC), one of the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors in ancient China, the qin began to have five strings to represent the five basic elements of the universe, namely, metal, wood, water, fire, and earth.

Then, King Wen (1152-1056 BC), the first ruler of the Zhou Dynasty (1046-256 BC), added a sixth string to the qin to mourn his son after he died. Therefore, people often find the sound of the sixth string of guqin sorrowful.

Again, King Wu (1087-1043 BC), the next ruler of the Zhou Dynasty, added the seventh string to inspire his soldiers as his country was going to war with the Shang Dynasty (1600-1046 BC). Therefore, this string often produces a masculinely resonant sound, representing bravery and courage.

Later, guqin became standardized as the one we see today and the instrument is full of symbolism of Chinese culture and philosophy.

For instance, the length of the body of guqin measures 3 chi 6.5 cun (old Chinese feet and inches, about 120-125 centimeters), to symbolize the 365 days of the year. Also, there are 13 inlaid dots of ivory, jade or mother-of-pearl on the outer edge of the guqin, which indicate the pitch positions and represent the 13 months in an intercalary lunar year.

Meanwhile, the slightly convex upper surface of the sound board, made of paulownia wood, is rounded, representing the sky and the bottom base, usually made of Chinese catalpa, is flat, symbolizing the earth. This is because ancient Chinese believed that the sky was round and the earth flat.

Therefore, the performance of guqin signifies the union of heaven, earth, and man.

In 1977, the US spaceship Voyager was launched into the outer space with a gold CD on board to introduce the music of this planet to the rest of the universe. A recording of “Flowing Water,” a traditional masterpiece of the guqin, performed by Guan Pinghu (1897-1967), one of the best guqin players of the 20th century, was included in the gold CD as one representative of the world’s music.

In 2003, guqin music was proclaimed as one of the Masterpieces of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO.

title: Play Chinese Guqin in Juntianfang, Daxing
description:
Beijing Juntianfang is a comprehensive guqin cultural center located in Daxing District, founded by guqin artist Wang Peng.

Its core features are as follows:

Exquisite Craftsmanship: Centered on Wang Peng's guqin-making skills, it strictly adheres to traditional methods while pursuing excellence in tonal quality and aesthetics, alongside guqin restoration work.

Systematic Heritage System: Offers professional training in guqin performance and craftsmanship, cultivating talents in both playing and making.

Diverse Cultural Platform: Integrates concert halls, exhibition spaces, and garden settings, regularly hosting concerts and elegant gatherings to promote a modern literati lifestyle centered on the guqin.

Cultural Dissemination Base: As a intangible cultural heritage base, it actively promotes the contemporary popularization and international exchange of guqin culture.

In summary, Juntianfang is not merely a guqin workshop but an integrated ecological platform for guqin production, research, education, and dissemination, serving as an important model for the revival of contemporary guqin art.

title: 寒江｜古琴與簫 | Ancient Chinese Guqin & Xiao | Zen Meditation | Relaxing Ambient Music
description:
【素心】系列 · 寒江
寒江無聲，孤舟緩行。
霧鎖長河，水天一線，萬籟俱寂。
七弦輕撥，如冰裂於靜夜；
一管簫音，隨霧遠去，不留痕跡。

江寒非為凜冽，
而是洗盡浮躁後的澄明。
此曲以古琴之沉靜、古箏之清冷、簫聲之虛遠交織，
如雪覆江面，留白勝言。
不問來處，不計歸途，
只在寒江之上，與自己靜靜相對。

願此音伴您遠離喧囂，
在霧與水之間，安住當下，
回歸「素心」本然之境。

🌫️ Solitude · Cold River

A silent river draped in mist,
a lone boat drifting slowly through the cold.
Guqin resonates with restrained depth,
Guzheng glimmers like frozen water,
while the Xiao dissolves into the fog.

This is not coldness, but clarity —
a stillness after all noise has faded.
No rhythm to follow, no destination to reach,
only sound flowing gently across an empty river.

May this music guide you into quiet awareness,
where thoughts settle like snow,
and the mind returns to its pure, unadorned state.

title: Starwatch 🌙 Ancient Chinese Guqin and Choir
description:
🎵 Ancient Chinese guqin floating above ethereal choir pads, woven with English and Chinese lyrics at 432Hz — a heavenly bilingual lullaby for the soul that cannot find rest. You are not alone
tonight. Let these voices find you.

Some nights the mind needs more than music. It needs to be reminded that peace is not something to find — it is something to return to. That is what this recording is for.

────────────────────────────────
🌙 PERFECT FOR:
- Deep sleep & insomnia relief
- Zen meditation practice
- Mindfulness & presence
- Stress and anxiety relief
- Morning or evening practice
- Japanese aesthetic appreciation
────────────────────────────────

🎼 LYRICS TRANSLATION
The Chinese lyrics translate as:
"Bamboo shadows sweep the steps but dust does not move — Moon passes through water leaving no trace — Wind through the pines leaves no sound — Return to stillness."

These are adapted from classic Chinese Zen poetry, over one thousand years old.

────────────────────────────────
Starwatch 🌙 Ancient Chinese Guqin and Choir

Above the veil of mortal dreaming
The stars keep watch with silent grace
Eternal light is softly streaming
Into this sacred resting place
Wandering souls find their way here
Drawn by an ancient, gentle call
In the arms of heaven drawing near
Every fear begins to fall

Oh, let the heavens embrace you
Let the light forever hold you
In this realm of endless peace
All is forgiven, all is released
You are home… you are home…

天光普照尘世外
(Tian guang pu zhao chen shi wai)
星辰为我开天门
(Xing chen wei wo kai tian men)
灵魂乘风归故里
(Ling hun cheng feng gui gu li)
忘却凡尘万般累

Oh, let the heavens embrace you
Let the ancient light enfold you
In this realm of endless peace
All is forgiven, all is released
You are home… you are home…

The guqin sings of timeless wonder
Strings of light across the sky
Every note pulls you asunder
From the weight of days gone by
Rest now child of the eternal
Let the choir lift your weary soul
In this vast and holy temple
You are finally, fully whole

Female: No more sorrow, no more night
Male: 归于光明 (Gui yu guang ming)
Female: Only beauty, only light
Male: 永恒安宁 (Yong heng an ning)

Oh, let the heavens embrace you
Let the divine forever hold you
In this ocean of pure grace
You are loved in every space
You are home… you are home…
You are home…

归家了…
(You have returned home…)

Wordless choir pads breathing slowly…
Single guqin note lingering…
Gentle return to nature sounds…
Silence.

title: 1 Hour Chinese Guqin Music for Study | Clear Lake, White Cranes, Guqin for Focus
description:
White cranes by a clear lake with guqin-led Chinese ambience for focus, reading, and study.
1 hour of bright wetland air, soft reed wind, calm water reflections, and a steady no-vocals soundscape.

This episode is designed for long focus sessions with a stable visual loop and low-distraction sound design. The music keeps one fixed ensemble throughout the full hour: guqin, ruan, guzheng harmonics, and xiao.

What you will hear:
- guqin, ruan, guzheng harmonics, and xiao
- bright lake ambience and wetland air
- light reed-bank movement
- no vocals
- no lyrics
- no strong percussion
- no dramatic climax

What you will see:
A painterly blue-green lake, distant mountains, golden reeds, and two white cranes holding a calm looped pose with subtle movement. The scene is fictional and atmospheric rather than documentary footage.

Use this for:
- deep focus
- reading
- study sessions
- quiet daytime work
- calm background listening

古琴演奏家：谷俊勇

谷俊勇，今年19岁，自12岁起 于青岛古琴协会学习古琴，后师从于中央音乐学院赵晓霞副教授，于2022年考入武汉音乐学院古琴表演专业，受教于古琴大家丁承运先生的女儿丁霓裳老师，目前大二在读。

title: Drunken Ecstasy 酒狂 - Chinese Guqin 古琴
description:
Jiu Kuang (Drunken Ecstasy) is one of the most iconic and expressive pieces in the Chinese guqin repertoire. Traditionally attributed to Ruan Ji, a poet-musician and one of the “Seven Sages of the Bamboo Grove” from the Wei-Jin period, the piece reflects a deep sense of emotional freedom and spiritual rebellion.

🎼 Performed by: Lin Qingjun
🏛️ Presented by: Zhe Nan GuQin Society
🎵 Piece: Jiu Kuang (Drunken Ecstasy)
《酒狂》是中国古琴中最具个性与精神张力的经典曲目之一，相传出自魏晋“竹林七贤”之一的阮籍之手。

此曲节奏奔放跳跃，旋律跌宕起伏，如醉非醉、若狂非狂，传达的是魏晋士人“借酒写志、纵情而不失本心”的自由精神与文化气度。

本曲由古琴演奏家林庆君老师演绎，指法洒脱而不失精微，展现出“狂中有道”的琴人之境。

🎼 演奏：林庆君
🏛️ 出品：浙楠琴社 Zhe Nan Qin Society
🎵 曲目：《酒狂》

title: 古琴名曲《離騒》Chinese Guqin
description:
《古琴名曲解說》：

题  解
战国末期，楚国贵族屈原志在兴楚安邦，拯救黎民。然屡遭谗言，忠贞被贬、大志难申，故作长篇抒情诗《离骚》以写其心。此诗以香草美人为喻，引用大量神话传说，表达了其洁身自好，不与邪恶同流合污，追随先贤之迹的高洁心胸。诗歌文采绚烂，构思宏伟，天上地下，游心无垠，独创骚体，开一代文宗。本曲以此诗为蓝本，以其主要章节为依托而写成，气势恢弘，曲韵曲折，自有一种正气与悲气弥漫，堪为琴中之“离骚”。

title: Traditional Chinese Music for Meditation | Beautiful Chinese Guqin Music | Feng
description:
Welcome to a world of peace and serenity with traditional Chinese music featuring the enchanting melodies of the Guqin. This video, titled "Traditional Chinese Music for Meditation | Beautiful Chinese Guqin Music", is a perfect blend of ancient Chinese culture and soothing music, ideal for meditation, mindfulness, and deep relaxation. The Guqin, a seven-stringed zither known for its rich history and spiritual essence, has been played for over 3,000 years and is deeply intertwined with Chinese philosophy, poetry, and the pursuit of tranquility.

In this video, you will experience the following:

🔹 Authentic Guqin Sounds: Enjoy the pure, resonant tones of the Guqin, which evoke a sense of stillness and inner peace. The delicate plucking of the strings creates a musical landscape that soothes the soul and invites introspection.

🔹 Beautiful Traditional Melody: The piece “Feng” is crafted to reflect the harmony between nature and the human spirit. The melody is soft yet expressive, capturing the essence of the wind (feng), as it gently sways through the valleys and mountains of ancient China.

🔹 Perfect for Mindfulness Practices: Use this music as a backdrop for meditation, yoga, or tai chi. The gentle rhythm and serene atmosphere can help reduce stress, calm the mind, and enhance your focus.

🔹 Background Music for Study & Work: Let this peaceful Guqin music accompany you during study sessions, work, or reading. Its calming effects can help improve concentration and create a peaceful environment free of distractions.

🔹 Cultural and Historical Significance: The Guqin is not just an instrument but a symbol of wisdom, elegance, and scholarly refinement. Listening to its music is like taking a journey back in time, experiencing the same peaceful sounds that inspired poets and scholars throughout the ages.

🎵 Credits & Inspiration: This piece is inspired by the classical Chinese philosophy of harmony between heaven, earth, and humanity. It reflects themes of nature, simplicity, and tranquility, drawing from the rich cultural heritage of traditional Chinese music.

💬 Feedback & Community: We'd love to hear your thoughts and experiences with this music. Feel free to share how it has helped you in your meditation or relaxation journey in the comments below!

📌 Subscribe to our channel for more traditional music, meditation soundtracks, and calming melodies that nurture your soul.

title: 《忆故人》Guqin Traditional Piece｜Ancient Chinese Music – Remembrance of Old Friends
description:
“空山寂寂，明月窗前，思君不见，忧思何限” 古琴曲《忆故人》，又名《山中思故人》，是近代广为流传的经典名曲。其乐谱最早见于清代，后经彭祉卿整理、查阜西演奏推广而成现今版本。乐曲属商调，音色天然苍凉，深情缠绵，被誉为琴乐抒情的典范。 全曲结构严谨，以空灵泛音开场，营造出空山静夜的静谧氛围。核心主题旋律婉转优美，通过多次变奏及“吟猱绰注”等技法层层推进，将思念之情从幽静怀想逐渐深化为心潮澎湃的高潮，最终复归于悠远绵的余韵，感人至深。把这个翻一下

Guqin: 南一先生
Recording / Video: 南一先生的先生

If you enjoy ancient Chinese music, guqin performances, or meditative instrumental soundscapes, welcome to subscribe for more traditional repertoire.“Within the empty mountains, silence prevails.
The bright moon hangs before the window.
I think of you, yet you cannot be seen—how boundless this sorrow becomes.”

Remembrance of Old Friends (《忆故人》), also known as Thinking of a Friend in the Mountains (《山中思故人》), is a classic guqin piece widely circulated in modern times. Its earliest surviving notation appears in the Qing Dynasty. The version commonly performed today was later compiled by Peng Zhihuang and promoted through the interpretation of master Zha Fuxi.

The piece is set in the shang mode, characterized by a naturally desolate yet deeply expressive tone, and is regarded as a model of lyrical expression in guqin music.

The composition is carefully structured. It begins with ethereal harmonics, evoking the stillness of an empty mountain night. The central theme unfolds with graceful and flowing melodic lines, gradually deepened through variations and traditional guqin techniques such as yin, nao, chuo, and zhu (vibrato, slides, and ornamental inflections).

The emotion evolves from quiet contemplation into an intense wave of longing, before finally returning to a distant and lingering resonance—leaving a profound and touching aftertaste.“空山寂寂，明月窗前，思君不见，忧思何限” 古琴曲《忆故人》，又名《山中思故人》，是近代广为流传的经典名曲。其乐谱最早见于清代，后经彭祉卿整理、查阜西演奏推广而成现今版本。乐曲属商调，音色天然苍凉，深情缠绵，被誉为琴乐抒情的典范。 全曲结构严谨，以空灵泛音开场，营造出空山静夜的静谧氛围。核心主题旋律婉转优美，通过多次变奏及“吟猱绰注”等技法层层推进，将思念之情从幽静怀想逐渐深化为心潮澎湃的高潮，最终复归于悠远绵的余韵，感人至深。把这个翻一下

description:
The Mysterious Chinese Guqin: Ancient Melodies for Wellness丨Calm Down and Relax丨Stop Overthinking

The guqin is the oldest plucked instrument in China, with a recorded history of more than 3,000 years. The sound of the guqin is quiet and gentle, and can express a wealth of emotions. The guqin is often used to express feelings, cultivate temperament, and is a tool for cultivating one's health.

The guqin's health-enhancing effects are introverted, subtle and far-reaching. Its unique musical characteristics can help people adjust their body and mind, harmonizing the balance of yin and yang with its unique rhythm. Its wonderful melody is like a clear spring that flows into one's heart and nourishes one's spirit.

Ancient Chinese believed that the guqin has the effect of cultivating the body, nourishing the mind and curing diseases, and that the rhythm of the guqin corresponds to the five organs and six bowels of the human body. The ebb and flow of the rhythm of the guqin resonates with one's inner being, which in turn has a profound effect on one's emotions and body. Each guqin piece is a unique energy that can guide people into a specific state of mind. In the process of listening to the guqin, people can gradually release their emotions and feelings under the guidance of the qin, achieving the purpose of harmonizing the balance of yin and yang.
