title: RELAXING GUQIN MUSIC | Ancient Chinese Qin | Stress Relief | Meditation, Study, and Sleep
description:
The guqin ([kùtɕʰǐn] (listen); Chinese: 古琴) is a plucked seven-string Chinese musical instrument. It has been played since ancient times, and has traditionally been favoured by scholars and literati as an instrument of great subtlety and refinement, as highlighted by the quote "a gentleman does not part with his qin or se without good reason,"[1] as well as being associated with the ancient Chinese philosopher Confucius. It is sometimes referred to by the Chinese as "the father of Chinese music" or "the instrument of the sages". The guqin is not to be confused with the guzheng, another Chinese long stringed instrument also without frets, but with moveable bridges under each string.
There are more than 3,360 known surviving pieces of guqin music from ancient and imperial periods. On 7 November 2003, UNESCO announced that the Chinese guqin was selected as an Intangible World Cultural Heritage. In 2006, guqin was listed in the List of National Non-material Cultural Heritage in China. In 2010, a Song period guqin was sold for $22 million, making it the most expensive musical instrument ever sold. (wikipedia).

title: 【古琴Guqin】宋琴·仙籟《流水》 "Flowing Water"——A thousand-year-old Chinese guqin touches the soul.
description:
攻玉山房 藏【宋琴·仙籟】連珠式
琴體寬長厚重，線條流暢。黑漆朱髹，通體斷紋，斑爛陸離，龍鱗，龜甲、流水、蛇腹、錯雜相間，美不勝收。漆下隱現八寶灰，恍似宋代「巨碑式」山水畫。
龍池鳳沼均為圓形，背頸刻有篆書「僊籟」二字是為琴名。名下方刻有字銘「雲璈弄之，玉調吟風，覺響屧鳴珂，銀鉦鐵馬，未足髣髴其逸韻之沖融」。
龍池下刻方章：「唐侃之印」、「寶敘齋」。唐侃，明確見於史籍為乾隆皇帝演奏的琴人，《活計檔》中奉旨為內務府定級古琴之人。乾隆禦製詩有《聽唐侃彈琴》：「仙籟平張梧幾橫，澄心試聽指弦聲」。

本視頻演奏曲目為古琴名曲《流水》：此曲現存最早的琴譜初見於1425年的《神奇秘譜》，題解雲：《高山》《流水》二曲本只一曲。初誌在乎高山，言仁者樂山之意。後誌在乎流水，言智者樂水之意。至唐分為兩曲，不分段數。至宋分《高山》為四段，《流水》為八段。按琴史列子雲，伯牙善鼓琴，鐘子期善聽。伯牙誌在高山，鐘子期曰，巍巍乎若泰山。伯牙誌在流水，鐘子期曰，洋洋乎若江海。伯牙所念，子期心明。伯牙曰，善哉，子之心而與吾心同。子期既死，伯牙絕弦，終身不復鼓也。故有高山流水之曲。而唐詩中已有《流水曲》《流水引》的提法，可見其淵源甚早，可追溯至唐代以前。
現今所流傳之《流水》，多為清末道士張孔山據《德音堂琴譜》中同名琴曲加工而成，收錄於其所編的《天聞閣琴譜》中。《沙堰琴編》雲:天聞閣流水艷稱海內，琴家每以不得其傳為恨，或有自雲其先人曾親受學孔山者，自藏鈔本寶而秘之。此後，又出現十來種版本，具言得自張孔山，然曲譜頗有出入，應是張孔山在加工此曲中不同階段的產物。
張孔山加工後的《流水》，增加了滾、拂、綽、註等指法以描寫水勢，因此也被稱為《七十二滾拂流水》。此曲音樂形象鮮明，時清清冷冷如松根之細流，時鏦鏦錚錚如幽澗之寒流，時又浩浩洋洋如江海之長流，極盡煙波浩渺，活潑瀠洄之致也。
現代琴家管平湖先生善奏此曲，其所奏之《流水》，取意高古，氣勢磅礴，享譽南北。1977年8月，美國發射的太空船探索者號錄製了一批各具特色的世界名曲，以尋求天外知音，管先生的《流水》作為東方音樂的代表入選。這首古老的樂曲，從此行雲天際，回響於茫茫太空。

古琴演奏：蔡珊 譜本：據《天聞閣琴譜》

description: The earliest known version of "Ping Sha Luo Yan" appears in the Ming Dynasty's "Shen Qi Mi Pu" (1425). The piece is traditionally attributed to the Tang Dynasty scholar and guqin player Shao Yong, though its exact origins remain uncertain. Over centuries, various schools of guqin playing, such as the Mei’an and Guangling schools, have developed their interpretations, resulting in slightly different versions of the piece.

臞仙曰，是曲者，周公之所作也。大槩周建邦本，贵乎同姓，以天下为家，以家人治之，故不致于异类之倾覆，而能开八百六十七年之国，致使享国最为悠久而不坠。按诗经云，雅者正也，正乐之歌也。其篇有大小之殊，先儒有正变之别，正小雅乃燕飨之乐，正大雅乃朝会之乐，受厘陈戒之辞，欢欣和悦，以尽群下之情，恭敬齐庄，以发先王之德，是以词气不同，音节亦异，致有大雅小雅之别。予故删其小雅燕飨者而不用，独取其大雅正声之音而订正之。

title: Ancient Chinese Guqin Music 🎋 Moonlit Pavilion & Misty Mountains | Relaxing Meditation Instrumental
description:
Escape into the tranquil beauty of Ancient China with this relaxing instrumental journey featuring the gentle sounds of the guqin, traditional Chinese melodies, and peaceful mountain ambience.

Travel through moonlit pavilions, floating lanterns, mist-covered mountains, sacred temples above the clouds, serene lotus lakes, and hidden waterfalls while soothing traditional-inspired music creates a calming atmosphere for relaxation, meditation, study, reading, sleep, yoga, and stress relief.

This cinematic visual experience is inspired by the timeless elegance of ancient Chinese landscapes and classical oriental aesthetics, bringing together peaceful nature, soft lantern light, and spiritual serenity.

Technique:
Tuo (托) — single thumb plucks outward on the bass strings, slow and spaced. Let each note ring fully before the next.
Fan yin (泛音 / harmonics) — touch the string lightly at its midpoint for bell-like overtones. These are your "lanterns" — tiny points of light against the dark water.
Keep tempo rubato (free), no metronome. Breathe between notes.

Perfect for:
• Relaxation & Stress Relief
• Meditation & Mindfulness
• Deep Focus & Studying
• Reading & Creative Work
• Sleep & Insomnia Relief
• Yoga & Healing Practices
• Peaceful Background Ambience

Let the gentle guqin melodies guide you through a world of moonlit mountains, drifting mist, and ancient temples hidden among the clouds.

🎶《卡门》哈巴涅拉三重奏版首演，编曲与指法移植 by 令辰，演奏by松籁·巴黎古琴协会成员：石雅蕾、邱知浅、黄雯静、陈思（鼓）、令辰。

🎼 2025 Sōnglài · Association Paris Guqin — Concert de maître Lingchen et ses élèves
📍 Enregistrement en direct · Fête de la Musique à Paris

Quatre guqins et un tambourin.
Avec cet instrument millénaire venu d’Orient, nous avons interprété la rébellion et la tendresse d’une femme espagnole.

« Qu’est-ce que l’amour ? Qu’est-ce que le désir ? »
Pour Carmen, c’est une liberté qui refuse d’être définie.
Sur les cordes du guqin, c’est une tendresse retenue, à peine murmurée.

🎶 Habanera de Carmen — première version en trio pour guqin
Arrangement et transcription : Lingchen
Interprétation : membres de l’Association Paris Guqin

title: 古琴名曲《平沙落雁》 Chinese Guqin
description:
《古琴自修進階》：

题解
秋高气爽、北雁南飞，茫茫平沙，何处可依！江潭月落、秋影横霜，风中一鸣，声断衡阳！这是一首以景写心之曲。借鸿鹄之高飞，写志士之心胸；以北雁之啼鸣，抒志士之不平；以雁阵之栖息不定，传志士之怀才不遇。此曲深得儒家乐教的中和之美，乐而不淫，哀而不伤，意境阔大、空灵，幽深无际。然此曲之最动人处，并不仅仅在于音乐的唯美，还有其深度的意象空间。他触及了深藏于每个人心底的对生命家园的追问，一种旷古的身世飘零之感。人生不过是一趟单向的旅程，在世世轮回的飘零中，何处才是生命的真正家园？就象南飞的雁群，年年往返、漂泊不定，暂落平沙，复又作万里长飞。古人云：声外之韵、象外之致，于此曲之中可窥见一二，一曲听罢，令人回味无穷、悠想万千。

EXILE | 卜算子 · Su Shi
1 Hour of Ancient Chinese Music for Deep Sleep, Meditation & Inner Peace

揀盡寒枝不肯棲，寂寞沙洲冷
"Refused every cold branch — perched alone on a desolate sandbar."

A guqin solo inspired by Su Shi's 卜算子, written c. 1080–1082 during his
exile to Huangzhou — composed alone in a monastery cell, the poet projecting
his own sleepless integrity onto a lone wild goose. The cold austerity of

title: Liu Shui (流水) – Guqin Performance | Chinese Traditional Music | Xiaochan Wang
description:
This is another performance video of “Liu Shui” (Flowing Water), one of the most celebrated masterpieces in the guqin repertoire.

“Liu Shui” vividly depicts the dynamic movement of water — from gentle streams to powerful, surging currents. The piece is known for its rich techniques and expressive storytelling, symbolizing both the vitality of nature and the depth of human emotion.

This performance was presented upon invitation from the Confucius Institute Bremen, as part of a cultural event sharing traditional Chinese music.

✨ Through this interpretation, I aim to highlight the contrast between stillness and motion, allowing the music to unfold like an ever-changing landscape of sound.

Performed by Xiaochan Wang, a guqin artist based in Germany, trained under the renowned master Li Pengpeng.

About the piece:

Title: Liu Shui (流水)
Style: Classical Chinese Guqin music
Theme: Nature, movement, and energy
Notable: One of the most famous guqin pieces, historically associated with the story of “High Mountains and Flowing Water” (知音)

If you enjoyed this performance, feel free to like, comment, and subscribe for more traditional Chinese music.

此曲又名春闰怨，是一首反映春愁闰怨的作品。同为描写春晓，此曲与《春晓吟》大异其趣。《春晓吟》抒发了人对春晓美景的无限悠想，而《玉楼春吟》则是伤春怀愁之作。作品刻画细腻，把一个闺中女子在春晓前的一段心理活动刻画的细致入微、哀婉动人。作品曲境幽深，曲韵婉转；如红颜坐愁，向人缓缓诉说一个令人伤感的往事。

title: 鸥鹭忘机_西村_Chinese Guqin Traditional Music
description:
問我何所來
天地橐龠中
造化育無窮
小大亦何空

海客談荒國
飛鳥覓魚蟲
十萬大千界
逍遙任飛鴻

悠悠興亡事
歷歷成敗名
英雄惜遲暮
宗愨慕長風

一曲鷗鷺勞忘機
人生百年心何迷
為賦新詞頻煎酒
悶上心來倦梳頭

自比蓬萊仙中仙
身逢盡是火焰山
不見大聖孫行者
醉向佛祖悟參禪

問君何所往
君心向遠方
臨行不得語
鷗鷺忽滿天

鷗鷺忘機——西村

title: Water Tranquility 水境 中國風音樂   Olden Chinese Guqin & Xiao Flute   Zen Meditation & Relaxation Audio
description:
霧深之處，天地無形。
音起之時，心念自止。

一人立於舟上，
不語，不動，
只是讓簫聲穿過濃霧，
落入水面與夜色之中。

此刻無需追尋、無需理解，
讓所有未說完的心事化入夜波，
讓未安定的念頭沉入霧後。

在聲音之外，
有更安靜的地方；
在呼吸之內，
有更柔軟的自己。

願你靜下來，
不求證悟，
只願安住。



When the mist grows deeper,
the world becomes softer.

When the Xiao begins,
the mind grows still.

Stand within the silence,
and let every breath
return to where it first began.

Here, nothing must be solved,
nothing must be reached.
Only stay—
and peace unveils itself.

title: Chinese GuQin традиционная китайская музыка
description:
https://theteahouse.ru/

Цисяньци́нь (кит. 七絃琴, ци «семь», сянь «струны», цинь «~струнный инструмент~»), также называют гуцинь (gǔqín 古琴) — китайский 7-струнный щипковый музыкальный инструмент.
Цисяньцинь — древнейший инструмент. В III веке до н. э. входил в состав придворного оркестра яюэ (雅樂, «изящная музыка») и был наиболее характерным инструментом классической древнекитайской музыки.
По принятой в Китае классификации (по материалу как источнику звука) относится к категории «шёлковых инструментов»).

title: 古琴名曲《漁樵問答》 Chinese Guqin
description:
《古琴名曲解說》：

题  解

古之贤者托渔樵以隐遁江湖，寄生命于山川风月。闲则聚于江畔、林下，谈古论今，吟诗作歌，如此笑傲红尘，好不惬意。本曲即是描摹上述场景的一首作品，通过渔樵之间的闲话对答，表达了作者对隐逸生活的推崇与向往，对山水之美的钟情。作者也借渔樵之乐，阐述了古之隐者的人生理念，充满道家的清幽与玄趣，超脱红尘之外，与自然融为一体。作品意境空灵、萧散闲适，冲和恬淡、天机一片。实乃养生之佳曲，弘道之妙音。

title: 古琴名曲《長門怨》Chinese Guqin
description:
《古琴名曲解說》：

题解
汉武帝刘彻的首任皇后陈阿娇，由于无子而失宠，以巫盅之术害人，事败被废，幽居长门宫中，以至终老。本曲以此为题，深度刻画了一个冷宫废后的孤凄生活及内心幽怨。曲情缠绵悱恻、哀宛动人，艺术手法极为成功。本曲与汉赋有《长门赋》有异曲同工之妙，或言此曲以《长门赋》为蓝本写就，亦可说通。此曲出于近世《梅庵琴谱》，最能代表诸城派琴曲的特点，曲韵体现出浓郁的山东方言特色。

title: 古琴名曲《漁 歌》Chinese Guqin
description:
《古琴名曲解說》：

题 解

柳宗元贬谪南荒，寓居永州。闲则仿渔人泛舟湖上、忘情山水。寻幽于千岩万壑之间，流连于烟霞水岸之际，暂忘世情，逍遥物外。归来作文以记之，同时亦作斯曲以传。曲境宛转幽奇、清逸深远，一任烟波、潇洒无碍。就像一组连续的风光视频，引领听者在山水间漫游，美不胜收。古人云：观画可作卧游，而听此曲更胜于观画，能让人一洗尘心，神游佳境。

If you enjoy the harmony of nature and the soothing vibes of ancient melodies, you’re in the right place. We hope this channel brings you a peaceful escape and a deeper appreciation for the beauty of Ancient Chinese music.

title: Chinese Guqin music, The Peaceful Night古琴曲 良宵引
description:
古琴曲《良宵引》是虞山琴派的代表曲目之一，是古琴初學者經常采用的入門琴曲，其曲譜最早見於明《松弦館琴譜》（1614），此書是虞山派的代表琴著，為《四庫全書》所收唯一的明代琴譜，在古琴界頗有影響。該曲自隋代問世，於明代盛行至今，一直深受琴家的贊賞和重視，并收錄在《大還閣琴譜》、《天聞閣琴譜》、《自遠堂琴譜》、《五知齋琴譜》、《琴學初津》、《詩夢齋琴譜》、《醒心琴譜》、《琴心直指》等多部琴著譜集中。

所謂「引」，是一種文體名，大略如序而稍為短簡。在音樂中，它是樂曲體裁之一，同時也代表了一種曲風，有序奏之意。從題意看，這是一首描寫月夜輕風，良宵雅興的琴曲。樂曲結構精致，旋律婉轉，曲風恬靜，引人入勝，是小曲之中的精品。

title: 【古琴Guqin】《离骚》 traditional Chinese music Li Sao
description:
离骚，中国古琴名曲，晚唐陈康士根据屈原同名抒情长诗《离骚》而作。曲谱最早见于《神奇秘谱》。原曲为九段，后人衍为十八段。此曲抒发了伟大爱国诗人屈原惨遭奸谗后的忧郁和苦闷，以及思乡爱国的崇高感情。历代古琴谱中超过四十部琴谱收录了此曲。

Lisao, a famous Chinese guqin song, was composed by Chen Kangshi in the late Tang Dynasty based on the long lyrical poem "Lisao" of the same name by Qu Yuan. The score was first seen in "Magic Secret Score". The original song has nine sections, and later generations have extended it to eighteen sections. This song expresses the melancholy and anguish of the great patriotic poet Qu Yuan after he was slandered, as well as his lofty feelings of homesickness and patriotism. More than 40 qin scores in the past dynasties have included this piece.

title: Summer Boating on Misty Lake | Guqin & Bamboo Flute Chinese Zen Music
description:
Welcome to Misty Lake, Calm Mind.

Today, we drift across a quiet summer lake.

A small wooden boat moves gently through clear blue water. Distant mountains fade into mist. A guqin rests beside the ripples, a bamboo flute carries the melody across the open lake, and Momo and Cloud keep quiet company under the soft morning light.

This peaceful Chinese instrumental music is created for calm focus, reading, studying, writing, meditation, gentle relaxation, and quiet rest. The mood is clear, spacious, elegant, and far-reaching — like taking a slow breath beside still water.

In this Misty Lake scene, Lin, Momo the orange cat, and Cloud the white cat return for a quiet lake journey filled with guqin, bamboo flute, misty mountains, soft reflections, and peaceful companionship.

May this music help your mind feel a little lighter, clearer, and calmer.

If this video brings you peace, I would be very grateful if you liked, subscribed, or left a comment. Your support helps Misty Lake, Calm Mind continue to grow.

Thank you for listening, and I hope you leave a little calmer than before.

title: 古琴《流水》Chinese Guqin: Flowing Streams · 秋水時至，百川灌河，涇流之大，兩涘渚崖之間，不辨牛馬。
description:
「善哉，善哉，子之聽夫志，想象猶吾心也。吾於何逃聲哉？」

“流水” (Flowing Streams) is a renowned melody in the guqin repertoire, deeply rooted in Chinese cultural heritage. Liezi (around BC400) documented this story about a legendary Guqin player Boya, who could evoke nature through his music. Only his friend Ziqi truly understood his melodies, leading to the expression “知音” (knowing one's sound), meaning friends who resonate with each other’s hearts.

Historically, “Flowing Streams” has been preserved in various handbooks from 1425 to 1946. The earliest version appears in the 1425 神奇秘譜 （Shen Qi Mi Pu）. Over the centuries, the melody evolved in multiple versions.The version of Liu Shui commonly played today makes extensive use of harp-like glissandos called "滾拂"（gunfu). This version is generally attributed to the 19th century Sichuan school master 張孔山 （Zhang Kongshan）, and it is commonly known as the "72 gunfu Liu Shui", This is by far the most popular version of Liu Shui, mostly because a recording of Guqin master 管平湖（Guan Pinghu） was selected for inclusion among the Voyager 2 recordings sent to outer space in 1977 as the representative of Chinese music . It well demonstrated Chinese Philosophy of Aesthetics and it is a symphony of human consciousness resonating with the Universe.

The music in this video is Guan Pinghu's version of Liushui, originally according to 天闻阁琴谱 handbook (1876). This video explores a new way of expressing the essence of Guqin and helping people who are not familiar with the instrument to enjoy the music via sounds, score, and motions.

title: 古琴名曲《古風操》Chinese Guqin music  悠悠古風  華夏之光
description:
《神奇秘谱》載：
臞仙曰：是曲者，古曲也，文王所作。其为趣也，追太古之淳风。谓不治而不乱，不言而自信，不化而自行。荡荡乎，民无能名焉。其俗也，熙熙然如登春台；以道存生，以德安形。其民甘食而乐居，怀土而重生，形有动作，心无好恶。鸡犬之声相闻，民至老死不相往来，无有好恶，无有嗜欲，此太朴之俗见於世也。

古之逸士、贤者，常托迹渔樵，隐遁无名、纵身大化。闲来泛舟湖上、随波任流，浩歌日月、醉卧烟波。此曲正是表达如此意境的一首佳作。斯人乘舟独往，或醉或醒、或歌或吟；览秋江、对明月；卧芦花、听落叶；荡舟扬波，此乐何极，羡煞红尘中人！欸乃者，舟上放歌之声。
